---
title: "Transfer Learning"
output: html_document
date: "2025-10-31"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}


knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
options(prType='html')
seed = 42
set.seed(seed)

options(error = function(){
  beepr::beep(3)
})

packages <- c(
    "beepr",
    "dplyr",
    "data.table",
    "knitr",
    "foreign",
    "tidyverse",
    "nhanesA",
    "xgboost",
    "caret",
    "viridis",
    "pROC",
    "ggplot2",
    "gridExtra"
)


for (pkg in packages) {
  # Check if the package is not already installed
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    # If not, install it from CRAN
    install.packages(pkg)
  }
  # Load the package into the R session
  library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
}


```

```{r}
#  Defining helper functions

# baseline diabetes label defined
make_bl_diabetes_label <- function(df,
                                  a1c = "LBXGH",
                                  fpg = "LBXGLU",
                                  ogt = "LBXGLT",
                                  out = "diabetes_flag") {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      has_a1c = !is.na(.data[[a1c]]),
      has_fpg = !is.na(.data[[fpg]]),
      has_ogt = !is.na(.data[[ogt]]),

      meets_a1c = has_a1c & .data[[a1c]] >= 6.5,
      meets_fpg = has_fpg & .data[[fpg]] >= 126,
      meets_ogt = has_ogt & .data[[ogt]] >= 200,

      any_obs  = has_a1c | has_fpg | has_ogt,
      any_meet = meets_a1c | meets_fpg | meets_ogt,

      "{out}" := case_when(
        any_meet            ~ 1,
        any_obs & !any_meet ~ 0,
        TRUE                ~ NA_real_
      )
    )
}

# encoding for categorical variables
# encode fit uses categorical variables from training df 
encode_fit <- function(df, cols) {
  maps <- lapply(cols, function(c) {
    v <- df[[c]]
    if (is.factor(v)) v <- as.character(v)
    levs <- sort(unique(as.character(v)))
    list(col = c, lut = setNames(seq_along(levs), levs))
  })
  names(maps) <- cols
  maps
}

# encode apply then applies this logic to any dataframe
encode_apply <- function(df, enc) {
  out <- df
  for (nm in names(enc)) {
    m <- enc[[nm]]
    v <- out[[m$col]]
    if (is.factor(v)) v <- as.character(v)
    out[[m$col]] <- unname(m$lut[as.character(v)])  # unseen variables become NA
  }
  out
}

# median imputing for missing values
# impute fit computes median for each column from training data
# impute_fit <- function(df, cols) {
#   meds <- vapply(cols, function(c) median(df[[c]], na.rm = TRUE), numeric(1))
#   list(medians = meds, cols = cols)
# }
# 
# # applys training median to any NAs in column
# impute_apply <- function(df, imp) {
#   out <- df
#   for (c in imp$cols) {
#     out[[c]][is.na(out[[c]])] <- imp$medians[[c]]
#   }
#   out
# }

# preprocessing to clean data
# # preprocessing fit to learn from training data 
# prep_fit <- function(train_df, feature_cols,
#                      cat_cols = c("RIAGENDR","RIDRETH1")) {
#   cat_cols <- intersect(cat_cols, feature_cols)
#   enc <- encode_fit(train_df, cat_cols)
#   train_enc <- encode_apply(train_df, enc)
#   imp <- impute_fit(train_enc, feature_cols)
#   list(enc = enc, imp = imp, cat_cols = cat_cols)
# }

# apply to every other df 
prep_apply_matrix <- function(df, prep, feature_cols) {
  df2 <- df
  if (length(prep$cat_cols) > 0) df2 <- encode_apply(df2, prep$enc)
  df2 <- impute_apply(df2, prep$imp)
  as.matrix(df2[, feature_cols, drop = FALSE])
}

# training xgb
# made so training for NHANES and DPPOS is identical 



# makes reproducible split for DPPOS
# random shuffle, 80/20 split
split_train_test <- function(df, prop = 0.8, seed = 42) {
  set.seed(seed)
  idx <- sample.int(nrow(df))
  cut <- floor(prop * length(idx))
  list(
    train = df[idx[1:cut], , drop = FALSE],
    test  = df[idx[(cut+1):length(idx)], , drop = FALSE]
  )
}
```


```{r New train code}
# ==========================================
# 2. The Upgraded Training Function
# ==========================================
# made so training for NHANES and DPPOS is identical, 
# but now includes automatic tuning!

train_xgb <- function(X_train, y_train, X_test, y_test, seed = 42) {
  
  # Convert to DMatrix format
  dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = X_train, label = y_train)
  dtest  <- xgb.DMatrix(data = X_test,  label = y_test)
  
  # Define the tuning grid
  search_grid <- expand.grid(
    max_depth = c(3, 4, 5),
    min_child_weight = c(1, 3),
    subsample = c(0.8),
    colsample_bytree = c(0.8),
    eta = c(0.05)
  )
  
  best_auc <- 0
  best_params <- list()
  best_nrounds <- 0
  
  cat("Running cross-validation to find optimal parameters...\n")
  
  
  # Grid Search Loop
  for (i in 1:nrow(search_grid)) {
    current_params <- list(
      objective = "binary:logistic",
      eval_metric = "auc",
      max_depth = search_grid$max_depth[i],
      min_child_weight = search_grid$min_child_weight[i],
      subsample = search_grid$subsample[i],
      colsample_bytree = search_grid$colsample_bytree[i],
      eta = search_grid$eta[i]
    )
    
    set.seed(seed)
    cv_results <- xgb.cv(
      params = current_params,
      data = dtrain,
      nrounds = 1000, 
      nfold = 5, 
      early_stopping_rounds = 20, 
      verbose = 0,
      maximize = TRUE
    )
    
    current_best_auc <- max(cv_results$evaluation_log$test_auc_mean)
    
    if (current_best_auc > best_auc) {
      best_auc <- current_best_auc
      best_params <- current_params
      best_nrounds <- cv_results$best_iteration
    }
  }
  
  cat("Tuning complete. Best CV AUC:", round(best_auc, 4), "at", best_nrounds, "rounds.\n")
  cat("Training final model...\n")
  
  # Train final model using the best discovered parameters
  model <- xgb.train(
    params = best_params,
    data = dtrain,
    nrounds = best_nrounds, # Use exact best rounds found in CV
    watchlist = list(train = dtrain, test = dtest),
    verbose = 0
  )
  
  # Predict and evaluate on the holdout test set
  pred <- predict(model, dtest)
  aucv <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(y_test, pred, quiet = TRUE))
  
  # Return the model, predictions, AUC, AND the best parameters
  list(model = model, pred = pred, auc = aucv, best_params = best_params)
}
```


```{r Old Train code}
# train_xgb <- function(X_train, y_train, X_test, y_test,
#                       params = list(
#                         objective = "binary:logistic",
#                         eval_metric = "auc",

#                       ),

#     params = params,
#     data = dtrain,
#     nrounds = nrounds,
#     watchlist = list(train = dtrain, test = dtest),
#     verbose = verbose
#   )
# 
#   pred <- predict(model, dtest)
#   aucv <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(y_test, pred, quiet = TRUE))
# 
#   list(model = model, pred = pred, auc = aucv)
# }


```




```{r Import NHANES 1999.2001.2003 files}
# data for 1999
# 
# demo_1999 <- nhanes("DEMO", 1999)
# bmx_1999 <- nhanesA::nhanes("BMX", 1999)
# bpx_1999 <-nhanes("BPX", 1999)
# mcq_1999 <- nhanes("MCQ", 1999)
# diq_1999 <- nhanes("DIQ", 1999)
# l10_1999 <- nhanes("LAB10", 1999)
# l10am_1999 <- nhanes("LAB10AM", 1999)
# lb25_1999 <- nhanes("LAB25", 1999)
# lb18_1999 <- nhanes("LAB18", 1999)
# bpq_1999 <- nhanes("BPQ", 1999)
# 
# bpx_1999$BPXSY <- rowMeans(
#   bpx_1999[, c("BPXSY1", "BPXSY2", "BPXSY3")],
#   na.rm = TRUE
# )
# 
# bpx_1999$BPXDI <- rowMeans(
#   bpx_1999[, c("BPXDI1", "BPXDI2", "BPXDI3")],
#   na.rm = TRUE
# )
# 
# vars.all.1999 <- c(
#   "SEQN","RIDAGEYR","RIAGENDR","BPXPLS","BPXSY","BPXDI","BMXHT","BMXWT","BMXWAIST","BMXBMI","RIDRETH1",
#   "BMXARMC","BPXPULS","BPQ020",
#   #"DIQ160","SLD012","DIQ180",
#   "MCQ080","MCQ092",
#   #"SLQ050",
#   "DIQ010",
#   #"DIQ170",
#   "MCQ250C","MCQ010","MCQ053","LBXNEPCT","LBXLYPCT","LBXMCVSI",
#   "LBXMOPCT","LBXEOPCT","LBXPLTSI","LBXMCHSI","LBXMPSI","LBXRBCSI","LBXHGB","LBXMC",
#   "LBXBAPCT","LBXWBCSI","LBXRDW","LBXSNASI","LBXSKSI","LBXSCLSI","LBXSC3SI","LBXSBU","LBXGLU",
#   "LBXSCR","LBXSCA","LBXSTP","LBXSTB","LBXSAL","LBXSASSI","LBXSATSI","LBXSAPSI","LBXGH","WTSAF2YR",
#   "WTMEC2YR"
# )
# 
# df_1999 <- reduce(
#   list(demo_1999, bmx_1999, bpx_1999, mcq_1999, diq_1999, l10_1999, l10am_1999, lb25_1999, lb18_1999, bpq_1999),
#   full_join,
#   by = "SEQN"
# ) %>%
#   select(any_of(vars.all.1999)) %>%
#   mutate(Year = 1999)
# 
# 
# 
# # importing, adjusting, cleaning data
# 
# demo_2001 <- nhanesA::nhanes("DEMO_B", 2001)
# bpx_2001 <- nhanesA::nhanes("BPX_B", 2001)
# bmx_2001 <- nhanesA::nhanes("BMX_B", 2001)
# l10_2001 <- nhanesA::nhanes("L10_B", 2001)
# l10am_2001 <- nhanesA::nhanes("L10AM_B", 2001)
# l25_2001 <- nhanesA::nhanes("L25_B", 2001)
# l40_2001 <- nhanesA::nhanes("L40_B", 2001)
# l40_2_2001 <- nhanesA::nhanes("L40_2_B", 2001)
# bpq_2001 <- nhanesA::nhanes("BPQ_B", 2001)
# diq_2001 <- nhanesA::nhanes("DIQ_B", 2001)
# mcq_2001 <- nhanesA::nhanes("MCQ_B", 2001)
# 
# bpx_2001$BPXSY <- rowMeans(
#   bpx_2001[, c("BPXSY1", "BPXSY2", "BPXSY3")],
#   na.rm = TRUE
# )
# 
# bpx_2001$BPXDI <- rowMeans(
#   bpx_2001[, c("BPXDI1", "BPXDI2", "BPXDI3")],
#   na.rm = TRUE
# )
# 
# vars.all.2001 <- c(
#   "SEQN","RIDAGEYR","RIAGENDR","BPXPLS","BPXSY","BPXDI","BMXHT","BMXWT","BMXWAIST","BMXBMI","RIDRETH1",
#   "BMXARMC","BPXPULS","BPQ020", "MCQ080","MCQ092","DIQ010", "MCQ250C","MCQ010",
#   "MCQ053","LBXNEPCT","LBXLYPCT","LBXMCVSI", "LBXMOPCT","LBXEOPCT","LBXPLTSI","LBXMCHSI",
#   "LBXMPSI","LBXRBCSI","LBXHGB","LBXMC","LBXBAPCT","LBXWBCSI","LBXRDW", "LBXGLU",
#   "LBXSNASI","LBXSKSI","LBXSCLSI","LBXSC3SI","LBXSBU","LBDSCR","LBXSCA","LBXSTP","LBDSTB",
#   "LBXSAL","LBXSASSI","LBXSATSI","LB2SAPSI","LBXGH","WTSAF2YR","WTMEC2YR"
# )
# 
# df_2001 <- list(
#   demo_2001,
#   bpx_2001,  bmx_2001,  l10_2001, l10am_2001, l25_2001, l40_2001,
#   l40_2_2001, bpq_2001,  diq_2001,  mcq_2001
# ) %>%
#   reduce(full_join, by = "SEQN")  %>%
#   select(any_of(vars.all.2001)) %>%
#   mutate(Year = 2001)
# 
# if ("LBDSCR" %in% names(df_2001)) {
#   df_2001 <- df_2001 %>% rename(LBXSCR = LBDSCR)
# }
# if ("LBDSTB" %in% names(df_2001)) {
#   df_2001 <- df_2001 %>% rename(LBXSTB = LBDSTB)
# }
# if ("LB2SAPSI" %in% names(df_2001)) {
#   df_2001 <- df_2001 %>% rename(LBXSAPSI = LB2SAPSI)
# }
# 
# demo_2003 <- nhanesA::nhanes("DEMO_C", 2003)
# bpx_2003 <- nhanesA::nhanes("BPX_C", 2003)
# bmx_2003 <- nhanesA::nhanes("BMX_C", 2003)
# l10_2003 <- nhanesA::nhanes("L10_C", 2003)
# l10am_2003 <- nhanesA::nhanes("L10AM_C", 2003)
# l25_2003 <- nhanesA::nhanes("L25_C", 2003)
# l40_2003 <- nhanesA::nhanes("L40_C", 2003)
# bpq_2003 <- nhanesA::nhanes("BPQ_C", 2003)
# diq_2003 <- nhanesA::nhanes("DIQ_C", 2003)
# mcq_2003 <- nhanesA::nhanes("MCQ_C", 2003)
# 
# bpx_2003$BPXSY <- rowMeans(
#   bpx_2003[, c("BPXSY1", "BPXSY2", "BPXSY3")],
#   na.rm = TRUE
# )
# 
# bpx_2003$BPXDI <- rowMeans(
#   bpx_2003[, c("BPXDI1", "BPXDI2", "BPXDI3")],
#   na.rm = TRUE
# )
# 
# vars.all.2003 <- c(
#   "SEQN","RIDAGEYR","RIAGENDR","BPXPLS","BPXSY","BPXDI","BMXHT","BMXWT","BMXWAIST","BMXBMI","RIDRETH1",
#   "BMXARMC","BPXPULS","BPQ020",
#   #"DIQ160","SLD012","DIQ180",
#   "MCQ080","MCQ092",
#   #"SLQ050",
#   "DIQ010",
#   #"DIQ170",
#   "MCQ250C", "MCQ010","MCQ053","LBXNEPCT","LBXLYPCT","LBXMCVSI",
#   "LBXMOPCT","LBXEOPCT","LBXPLTSI","LBXMCHSI","LBXMPSI","LBXRBCSI","LBXHGB","LBXMC",
#   "LBXBAPCT","LBXWBCSI","LBXRDW","LBXSNASI","LBXSKSI","LBXSCLSI","LBXSC3SI","LBXSBU", "LBXGLU",
#   "LBXSCR","LBXSCA","LBXSTP","LBXSTB","LBXSAL","LBXSASSI","LBXSATSI","LBXSAPSI","LBXGH","WTSAF2YR",
#   "WTMEC2YR"
# )
# 
# df_2003 <- list(
#   demo_2003,
#   bpx_2003,  bmx_2003,  l10_2003, l10am_2003, l25_2003, l40_2003,
#   bpq_2003,  diq_2003,  mcq_2003
# ) %>%
#   reduce(full_join, by = "SEQN")  %>%
#   select(any_of(vars.all.2003)) %>%
#   mutate(Year = 2003)
# 
# 
# unique_columns <- unique(c(names(df_1999), names(df_2001), names(df_2003)))
# 
# 
# df_1999_final <- df_1999 %>% select(any_of(unique_columns))
# df_2001_final <- df_2001 %>% select(any_of(unique_columns))
# df_2003_final <- df_2003 %>% select(any_of(unique_columns))
# 
# df_99.04 <- bind_rows(df_1999_final, df_2001_final, df_2003_final)
# 
# df_99.04 %>% select(LBXGLU,Year) %>% mutate(Exist=!is.na(LBXGLU)) %>% select(Exist,Year)%>% table()
# 
# df_99.04 %>% select(Year, RIAGENDR) %>% table()
# 
# saveRDS(object = df_99.04,file = "~/Documents/NHANES-DMDx/df_99.04.rds")
```


```{r Load df_99.04 RDS}

df_99.04 <- readRDS(file = "~/Documents/NHANES-DMDx/df_99.04.rds")

```

```{r}
dim(df_99.04)
```



```{r Build Dataset 2005-2016}
# building NHANES master dataset
# output is df_full
# 
# letters <- c('D','E','F','G','H','I')
# years   <- c(2005,2007,2009,2011,2013,2015)
# 
# vars <- c('DEMO','CBC','ALQ','BPQ','DIQ','MCQ',
#                     'BPX','BPXO','GHB','BMX','SLQ','HEPA',
#                     'HEPB_S','HEPE','FASTQX','OGTT','GLU','INS','BIOPRO','HEPBD','ALB_CR','HDL','TRIGLY','TCHOL')
# cols <- c('BMIRECUM','MCQ240D','BPAEN1','MCQ240L','MCQ240B',
#           'DIQ175A','DMDHHSIZ','DMDFMSIZ','DMDHHSZA','DMDHHSZB','DMDHHSZE')
# 
# no.var.let <- c('BPXO_D','BPXO_E','BPXO_F','BPXO_G','BPXO_H',
#                 'DXXAG_E','DXXAG_F','FERTIN_G','FERTIN_H','MGX_D',
#                 'MGX_E','MGX_F','HEPE_D','HEPE_E','INS_D',
#                 'INS_E','INS_F','INS_G')
# 
# 
# library(dplyr)
# library(purrr)
# 
# 
# fetch_and_clean <- function(var, letter, year) {
#   var_let <- if (letter == "P") paste0(letter, "_", var) else paste0(var, "_", letter)
#   if (var_let %in% no.var.let) return(NULL)
# 
#   df <- tryCatch(nhanes(var_let), error = function(e) NULL)
#   if (is.null(df)) return(NULL)
# 
#   # 1. Standardize column names to uppercase (NHANES sometimes mixes cases)
#   names(df) <- toupper(names(df))
# 
#   # 2. Add metadata
#   df <- df %>% mutate(Year = year, Source_File = var_let)
# 
#   # 3. Specific Logic for HEPBD
#   if (var_let %in% c("HEPBD_D", "HEPBD_E", "HEPBD_F")) {
#     df <- df %>% mutate(LBDHD = factor(LBDHD, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Positive", "Negative")))
#   }
# 
#   # 4. THE FIX: Force everything to character to allow binding
#   df <- df %>% mutate(across(everything(), as.character))
# 
#   return(df)
# }
# 
# # Run
# 
# final_list <- map(vars, function(v) {
#   map2(letters, years, ~fetch_and_clean(v, .x, .y)) %>%
#     compact() %>%
#     bind_rows() %>%
#     # 5. Optional: Try to convert numeric-looking columns back to double automatically
#     type_convert(guess_integer = TRUE)
# })
# 
# names(final_list) <- vars
# final_list
# 
# 
# 
# # 1. Clean the list: Ensure SEQN and Year are present and uppercase
# cleaned_list <- final_list %>%
#   map(function(df) {
#     if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)
# 
#     # Force all names to uppercase to fix seqn vs SEQN
#     names(df) <- toupper(names(df))
# 
#     # Ensure "YEAR" is uppercase too (it was added manually in our previous step)
#     if ("YEAR" %in% names(df)) {
#       # No action needed
#     } else if ("year" %in% names(df)) {
#       df <- df %>% rename(YEAR = year)
#     }
# 
#     # Only keep the dataframe if it actually has SEQN
#     if (!"SEQN" %in% names(df)) {
#       warning("A dataset is missing SEQN and will be excluded from the join.")
#       return(NULL)
#     }
# 
#     return(df)
#   }) %>%
#   compact() # Remove any NULLs
# 
# # 2. Perform the Join
# # We join by "SEQN" and "YEAR" (now both uppercase)
# master_df <- cleaned_list %>%
#   reduce(full_join, by = c("SEQN", "YEAR")) %>%
#   type_convert()
# 
# 
# library(dplyr)
# library(stringr)
# 
# master_df <- master_df %>%
#   mutate(
#     # Extract the first 4-digit number found in the text
#     Year = as.numeric(str_extract(SDDSRVYR, "\\d{4}"))
#   ) %>% relocate(SEQN,Year)

```





```{r}
# df.all <- master_df
# 
# df.all <- df.all %>%
#   mutate(
#     BPXSY = rowMeans(select(., dplyr::any_of(c("BPXSY1","BPXSY2","BPXSY3"))), na.rm = TRUE),
#     BPXDI = rowMeans(select(., dplyr::any_of(c("BPXDI1","BPXDI2","BPXDI3"))), na.rm = TRUE)
#   ) %>%
#   mutate(
#     BPXSY = ifelse(is.nan(BPXSY), NA_real_, BPXSY),
#     BPXDI = ifelse(is.nan(BPXDI), NA_real_, BPXDI)
#   ) %>%
#   select(-dplyr::any_of(c("BPXSY1","BPXSY2","BPXSY3","BPXDI1","BPXDI2","BPXDI3")))
# 
# df_full <- df.all
# 
# df_full <- df_full %>%
#   mutate(
#     LBXGLU = if_else(Year < 2007 & !is.na(LBXGLU), LBXGLU + 1.148, LBXGLU),
#     LBXGLT = if_else(Year < 2007 & !is.na(LBXGLT), LBXGLT + 1.148, LBXGLT)
#   ) %>%
#   mutate(
#     LBXGLU = if_else(Year < 2015 & !is.na(LBXGLU), 1.023 * LBXGLU - 0.5108, LBXGLU),
#     LBXGLT = if_else(Year < 2015 & !is.na(LBXGLT), 1.023 * LBXGLT - 0.5108, LBXGLT),
#     LBXSCR = if_else(Year == 2005 & !is.na(LBXSCR), -0.016 + 0.978 * LBXSCR, LBXSCR)
#   )
# 
# saveRDS(object=df_full, file="~/Documents/Data/NHANES/df_nhanes_2026.01.19.Rda")

df_full <-readRDS(file="~/Documents/Data/NHANES/df_nhanes_2026.01.19.Rda")
table(df_full$Year)




```



```{r}
dim(df_full)

```


```{r}
# building NHANES modeling dataset (df_model1)

df_model1 <- df_full %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    BMI = BMXWT / ((BMXHT/100)^2),

    has_a1c = !is.na(LBXGH),
    has_fpg = !is.na(LBXGLU),
    has_ogt = !is.na(LBXGLT),

    meets_a1c = has_a1c & LBXGH  >= 6.5,
    meets_fpg = has_fpg & LBXGLU >= 126,
    meets_ogt = has_ogt & LBXGLT >= 200,

    any_obs  = has_a1c | has_fpg | has_ogt, #### NA | NA | TRUE = TRUE; NA | NA | FALSE = FALSE
    any_meet = meets_a1c | meets_fpg | meets_ogt, #### 

    diabetes_flag = dplyr::case_when(
      any_meet             ~ 1,
      any_obs & !any_meet  ~ 0,
      TRUE                 ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(diabetes_flag)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    # Participant & Demographic Info
    #Age = RIDAGEYR,
    #Gender = RIAGENDR,

    # Blood Pressure Examination
    pulse = BPXPLS,
    #reg.iregg = BPXPULS,
    #SBP = BPXSY,
    #DBP = BPXDI,

    # Body Measures Examination
    #Height.Stand = BMXHT,
    #Weight = BMXWT,
    #Waist.Circ = BMXWAIST,
    #Arm.Circ = BMXARMC,

    # Questionnaire Data
    #BP.Ever.Told.High = BPQ020,
    #Ever.Told.Overweight = MCQ080,
    #TransfusionEver = MCQ092,
    #dm.told = DIQ010,
    #Told.Asthma = MCQ010,
    #Anemia.Treatment = MCQ053,
    # Cholesterol data
    
    # Complete Blood Count (CBC) Lab Data
    wbc = LBXWBCSI,
    #Lymph.Perc = LBXLYPCT,
    #Monocyte.perc = LBXMOPCT,
    #Seg.Neut.perc = LBXNEPCT,
    #eosin.perc = LBXEOPCT,
    #Baso.Perc = LBXBAPCT,
    rbc = LBXRBCSI,
    hg = LBXHGB,
    mcv = LBXMCVSI,
    #Mean.Cell.Hemoglobin = LBXMCHSI,
    #MCHC = LBXMC,
    RBC.Dist.Width = LBXRDW,
    plt = LBXPLTSI,
    #mpv = LBXMPSI,

    # Standard Chemistry Panel Lab Data
    na = LBXSNASI,
    potas = LBXSKSI,
    chlor = LBXSCLSI,
    bicarb = LBXSC3SI,
    bun = LBXSBU,
    #cr = LBXSCR,
    cal = LBXSCA,
    tprot = LBXSTP,
    alb = LBXSAL,
    tbili = LBXSTB,
    ast = LBXSASSI,
    alt = LBXSATSI,
    alp = LBXSAPSI,

    # Diabetes Markers Lab Data
    #fpg = LBXGLU,
    #a1c = LBXGH,
    #two = LBXGLT,

    # Survey Weights
    sample.ogtt.weight = WTSOG2YR,
    sample.fasting.weight = WTSAF2YR,
    sample.mec.weight = WTMEC2YR
  ) %>% 
  
  dplyr::select(
    Year,
    RIDAGEYR, RIAGENDR, RIDRETH1, #INDFMPIR, DMDHHSIZ,
    BMXHT, BMXWT, BMXWAIST, BPXSY, BPXDI, #BMI
    # LBDHDD, LBXTC, LBDLDL, #cholesterol
    LBXSCR,
    LBXGH,      # A1C
    LBXGLU,     # FPG
    LBXGLT,     # OGTT (for analysis)
    has_a1c, has_fpg, has_ogt,
    meets_a1c, meets_fpg, meets_ogt,
    diabetes_flag,
    # Participant & Demographic Info
    #Age = RIDAGEYR,
    #Gender = RIAGENDR,

    # Blood Pressure Examination
    pulse,# = BPXPLS,
    #reg.iregg = BPXPULS,
    #SBP = BPXSY,
    #DBP = BPXDI,

    # Body Measures Examination
    #Height.Stand = BMXHT,
    #Weight = BMXWT,
    #Waist.Circ = BMXWAIST,
    #Arm.Circ = BMXARMC,

    # Questionnaire Data
    #BP.Ever.Told.High = BPQ020,
    #Ever.Told.Overweight = MCQ080,
    #TransfusionEver = MCQ092,
    #dm.told = DIQ010,
    #Told.Asthma = MCQ010,
    #Anemia.Treatment = MCQ053,
    # Cholesterol data
    
    # Complete Blood Count (CBC) Lab Data
    wbc,# = LBXWBCSI,
    #Lymph.Perc,# = LBXLYPCT,
    #Monocyte.perc,# = LBXMOPCT,
    #Seg.Neut.perc,# = LBXNEPCT,
    #eosin.perc,# = LBXEOPCT,
    #Baso.Perc,# = LBXBAPCT,
    rbc,# = LBXRBCSI,
    hg,# = LBXHGB,
    mcv,# = LBXMCVSI,
    #Mean.Cell.Hemoglobin,# = LBXMCHSI,
    #MCHC,# = LBXMC,
    #RBC.Dist.Width,# = LBXRDW,
    plt,# = LBXPLTSI,
    #mpv,# = LBXMPSI,

    # Standard Chemistry Panel Lab Data
    na,# = LBXSNASI,
    potas,# = LBXSKSI,
    chlor,# = LBXSCLSI,
    bicarb,# = LBXSC3SI,
    bun,# = LBXSBU,
    #cr = LBXSCR,
    cal,# = LBXSCA,
    tprot,# = LBXSTP,
    alb,# = LBXSAL,
    tbili,# = LBXSTB,
    ast,# = LBXSASSI,
    alt,# = LBXSATSI,
    alp,# = LBXSAPSI,

    # Diabetes Markers Lab Data
    #fpg = LBXGLU,
    #a1c = LBXGH,
    #two = LBXGLT,

    # Survey Weights
    sample.ogtt.weight,# = WTSOG2YR,
    sample.fasting.weight,# = WTSAF2YR,
    sample.mec.weight,# = WTMEC2YR
    SEQN
  ) %>% filter(has_a1c==TRUE & has_fpg==TRUE & has_ogt==TRUE & !is.na(sample.ogtt.weight))# %>% filter(LBXGH<6.5,LBXGLU<126)
```
We included people who had A1c measurements.

```{r}
df_full
```


```{r}
df_model1 %>% select(diabetes_flag,has_a1c) %>% table()
```


```{r}
# breakdown of how many people in df_model1 meet/don't meet diabetes criteria
print(table(df_model1$diabetes_flag))
# as a proportion
print(round(prop.table(table(df_model1$diabetes_flag)), 3))
```



```{r}


library(htmlTable)
library(Gmisc)


df_table <- df_model1 %>% select(
   Year,
    RIDAGEYR, RIAGENDR, RIDRETH1, #INDFMPIR, DMDHHSIZ,
    BMXHT, BMXWT,  BMXWAIST, BPXSY, BPXDI, #BMI
    # LBDHDD, LBXTC, LBDLDL, #cholesterol
    LBXSCR,
    LBXGH,      # A1C
    LBXGLU,     # FPG
    LBXGLT,     # OGTT (for analysis)

    diabetes_flag,
 
    pulse,# = BPXPLS,

    wbc,# = LBXWBCSI,

    rbc,# = LBXRBCSI,
    hg,# = LBXHGB,
    mcv,# = LBXMCVSI,

    #RBC.Dist.Width,# = LBXRDW,
    plt,# = LBXPLTSI,

    na,# = LBXSNASI,
    potas,# = LBXSKSI,
    chlor,# = LBXSCLSI,
    bicarb,# = LBXSC3SI,
    bun,# = LBXSBU,
    cal,# = LBXSCA,
    tprot,# = LBXSTP,
    alb,# = LBXSAL,
    tbili,# = LBXSTB,
    ast,# = LBXSASSI,
    alt,# = LBXSATSI,
    alp,SEQN# = LBXSAPSI,
) %>% mutate(Type=ifelse(Year <2015,"Training","Testing"))


df_table %>% getDescriptionStatsBy(by=Type, names(df_table),
#`Fasting Glucose Plasma&dagger;`=fpg,				
add_total_col = FALSE) #%>% 
 # addHtmlTableStyle(pos.caption = "bottom") #%>% 
  #htmlTable(caption  = "Basic descriptive statistics from the NHANES dataset",
   #         tfoot = "&dagger; mg/dL")


saveRDS(object = df_table,file="~/Documents/NHANES-DMDx/df_table.Rds")


```


```{r}

df_model1 <- df_model1 %>% select(-SEQN)
```


```{r}
df_model1 %>% summarise(a1c=mean(has_a1c),fpg=mean(has_fpg),two=mean(has_ogt))
#df_model1 <- df_model1 %>% filter(!is.na(LBXGH))
```


```{r}
# define feature columns 
# does NOT include OGTT 
# includes A1c and FPG

feature_cols <-  names(df_model1)
feature_cols <- feature_cols[!feature_cols %in% c("Year", "has_a1c", "has_fpg", "has_ogt", "meets_a1c", 
"meets_fpg", "meets_ogt","diabetes_flag","sample.ogtt.weight","sample.fasting.weight","sample.mec.weight","LBXGLT")]

feature_cols.noFPG <- feature_cols[feature_cols!="LBXGLU"]

# check to make sure it is a binary outcome
# stopifnot(length(unique(df_model1$diabetes_flag)) == 2)

df_model1 <- df_model1 %>% mutate(RIDRETH1 = case_match(RIDRETH1,
    "Non-Hispanic Black" ~ "2",
    "Other Hispanic" ~ "3",
    "Other Race - Including Multi-Racial" ~ "4",
    "Non-Hispanic White" ~ "1",
    "Mexican American" ~ "3",
    .default = "Other"
  ))



```

```{r}
train_model1 <- df_model1 %>% dplyr::filter(Year < 2015)# %>% dplyr::filter((!is.na(LBXGH) & LBXGH<6.5))#,(!is.na(LBXGLU) & LBXGLU<126))
test_model1  <- df_model1 %>% dplyr::filter(Year >= 2015)  #%>% dplyr::filter((!is.na(LBXGH) & LBXGH<6.5))#,(!is.na(LBXGLU) & LBXGLU<126))

cat("Train participants by year:\n")
print(table(train_model1$Year))

cat ("\n")

cat("Test participants by year:\n")
print(table(test_model1$Year))

cat ("\n")

cat("Diabetes prevalence for training set :\n")
print(round(prop.table(table(train_model1$diabetes_flag)), 3))

cat ("\n")

cat("Diabetes prevalence for test set:\n")
print(round(prop.table(table(test_model1$diabetes_flag)), 3))


```



```{r}
# preprocessing
# preparing for xgboost 
# fit on TRAIN only, apply to test and train

encode_fit <- function(df, cols) {
  maps <- lapply(cols, function(c) {
    v <- df[[c]]
    if (is.factor(v)) v <- as.character(v)
    levs <- sort(unique(as.character(v)))
    list(col = c, levels = levs, lut = setNames(seq_along(levs), levs))
  })
  names(maps) <- cols
  maps
}

encode_apply <- function(df, enc) {
  for (nm in names(enc)) {
    m <- enc[[nm]]
    v <- df[[m$col]]
    if (is.factor(v)) v <- as.character(v)
    df[[m$col]] <- unname(m$lut[as.character(v)])
  }
  df
}

cat_cols <- intersect(c("RIAGENDR","RIDRETH1"), feature_cols)

if (length(cat_cols) > 0)
  {
  enc <- encode_fit(train_model1, cat_cols)
  train_model1 <- encode_apply(train_model1, enc)
  test_model1  <- encode_apply(test_model1,  enc)
  }


# impute_fit <- function(df, cols) {
#   meds <- vapply(cols, function(c) median(df[[c]], na.rm = TRUE), numeric(1))
#   list(medians = meds, cols = cols)
# }
# 
# impute_apply <- function(df, imp) {
#   for (c in imp$cols) {
#     df[[c]][is.na(df[[c]])] <- imp$medians[[c]]
#   }
#   df
# }
# imputing on train
# apply to train and test

#imp <- impute_fit(train_model1, feature_cols)
#train_model1 <- impute_apply(train_model1, imp)
#test_model1  <- impute_apply(test_model1,  imp)
``` 


```{r}
train_model1 
test_model1 %>% summarise(a1c=mean(is.na(LBXGH)),fpg=mean(is.na(LBXGLU)))
```

```{r New A1c+ } 
# ==========================================
# 3. Execution for Model 1 (NHANES)
# ==========================================

# Prepare matrices using your feature columns
X_train_mat <- as.matrix(train_model1[, feature_cols.noFPG])
y_train_vec <- train_model1$diabetes_flag

X_test_mat <- as.matrix(test_model1[, feature_cols.noFPG])
y_test_vec <- test_model1$diabetes_flag

# Call the new smart function
results_model1 <- train_xgb(
  X_train = X_train_mat,
  y_train = y_train_vec,
  X_test  = X_test_mat,
  y_test  = y_test_vec
)

pred_prob1 <- results_model1$pred
auc_val1 <- results_model1$auc
xgb_model1 <- results_model1$model
# Extract and print results
cat("\n==========================================\n")
cat("A1c+ NHANES Model (Internal Validation) AUC (Train=2005–2013, Test=2015) =", 
    round(auc_val1, 3), "\n")
cat("Parameters used:\n")
print(results_model1$best_params)
```


```{r Old A1c+}
# dtrain.1 <- xgb.DMatrix(
#   data  = as.matrix(train_model1[, feature_cols.noFPG]),
#   label = train_model1$diabetes_flag
# )
# 
# dtest.1 <- xgb.DMatrix(
#   data  = as.matrix(test_model1[, feature_cols.noFPG]),
#   label = test_model1$diabetes_flag
# )
# 
# params <- list(
#   objective = "binary:logistic",
#   eval_metric = "auc",
#   eta = 0.05,
#   max_depth = 4,
#   subsample = 0.8,
#   colsample_bytree = 0.8
# )
# 
# watchlist <- list(train = dtrain.1, test = dtest.1)
# 
# # train model 
# 
# xgb_model1 <- xgb.train(
#   params = params,
#   data = dtrain.1,
#   nrounds = 300,
#   watchlist = watchlist,
#   verbose = 0
# )
# 
# pred_prob1 <- predict(xgb_model1, dtest.1)
# 
# # Model 1 
# # internal validation
# 
# auc_val1 <- pROC::auc(test_model1$diabetes_flag, pred_prob1)
# cat("NHANES Model (Internal Validation) AUC (Train=2005–2013, Test=2015) =",
#     round(as.numeric(auc_val1), 3), "\n")

```

```{r}
# ==========================================
# 3. Execution for Model 1 (NHANES)
# ==========================================

# Prepare matrices using your feature columns
X_train_mat <- as.matrix(train_model1[, feature_cols])
y_train_vec <- train_model1$diabetes_flag

X_test_mat <- as.matrix(test_model1[, feature_cols])
y_test_vec <- test_model1$diabetes_flag

# Call the new smart function
results_model2 <- train_xgb(
  X_train = X_train_mat,
  y_train = y_train_vec,
  X_test  = X_test_mat,
  y_test  = y_test_vec
)

pred_prob2 <- results_model2$pred
auc_val2 <- results_model2$auc
xgb_model2 <- results_model2$model
# Extract and print results
cat("\n==========================================\n")
cat("A1c/FPG+ NHANES Model (Internal Validation) AUC (Train=2005–2013, Test=2015) =", 
    round(auc_val2, 3), "\n")
cat("Parameters used:\n")
print(results_model2$best_params)
```


```{r A1c/FPG+}
# dtrain.2 <- xgb.DMatrix(
#   data  = as.matrix(train_model1[, feature_cols]),
#   label = train_model1$diabetes_flag
# )
# 
# dtest.2 <- xgb.DMatrix(
#   data  = as.matrix(test_model1[, feature_cols]),
#   label = test_model1$diabetes_flag
# )
# 
# params <- list(
#   objective = "binary:logistic",
#   eval_metric = "auc",
#   eta = 0.05,
#   max_depth = 4,
#   subsample = 0.8,
#   colsample_bytree = 0.8
# )
# 
# watchlist <- list(train = dtrain.2, test = dtest.2)
# 
# # train model 
# 
# xgb_model2 <- xgb.train(
#   params = params,
#   data = dtrain.2,
#   nrounds = 1000,
#   early_stopping_rounds = 20,
#   watchlist = watchlist,
#   verbose = 0
# )
# 
# pred_prob2 <- predict(xgb_model2, dtest.2)
# 
# # Model 1 
# # internal validation
# 
# auc_val2 <- pROC::auc(test_model1$diabetes_flag, pred_prob2)
# cat("NHANES Model (Internal Validation) AUC (Train=2005–2013, Test=2015) =",
#     round(as.numeric(auc_val2), 3), "\n")

```
```{r}
saveRDS(object = xgb_model1,file="~/Documents/NHANES-DMDx/xgb_model1_2026.03.05.rds")

saveRDS(object = xgb_model2,file="~/Documents/NHANES-DMDx/xgb_model2_2026.03.05.rds")

```


```{r}
# comparison with other predictors

library(pROC)

train_eval <- train_model1 %>% dplyr::filter(has_a1c, has_fpg)
test_eval  <- test_model1  %>% dplyr::filter(has_a1c, has_fpg)

cat("N in train_eval:", nrow(train_eval), "\n")
cat("N in test_eval :", nrow(test_eval), "\n")

#glm_a1c <- LBXGH# glm(
#  diabetes_flag ~ LBXGH,
#  data   = train_eval,
#  family = binomial
#)
# 
# glm_fpg <- glm(
#   diabetes_flag ~ LBXGLU,
#   data   = train_eval,
#   family = binomial
# )

glm_both <- glm(
  diabetes_flag ~ LBXGH + LBXGLU,
  data   = train_eval,
  family = binomial
)


x_mat_test1 <- as.matrix(test_eval[, feature_cols.noFPG])
pred_xgb.1  <- predict(xgb_model1, x_mat_test1)

x_mat_test2 <- as.matrix(test_eval[, feature_cols])
pred_xgb.2  <- predict(xgb_model2, x_mat_test2)

# logistic model predictions for benchmark predictors
pred_a1c   <- test_eval$LBXGH #a1c#predict(glm_a1c,  newdata = test_eval, type = "response")
pred_fpg   <- test_eval$LBXGLU #predict(glm_fpg,  newdata = test_eval, type = "response")
pred_both  <- predict(glm_both, newdata = test_eval, type = "response")


# ROC curves
roc_xgb.1  <- pROC::roc(test_eval$diabetes_flag, pred_xgb.1,  quiet = TRUE)
roc_xgb.2  <- pROC::roc(test_eval$diabetes_flag, pred_xgb.2,  quiet = TRUE)
roc_a1c.nh  <- pROC::roc(test_eval$diabetes_flag, pred_a1c,  quiet = TRUE)
roc_fpg.nh  <- pROC::roc(test_eval$diabetes_flag, pred_fpg,  quiet = TRUE)
roc_both <- pROC::roc(test_eval$diabetes_flag, pred_both, quiet = TRUE)

# AUC 
auc_xgb.1  <- pROC::auc(roc_xgb.1)
auc_xgb.2  <- pROC::auc(roc_xgb.2)
auc_a1c.nh  <- pROC::auc(roc_a1c.nh)
auc_fpg.nh  <- pROC::auc(roc_fpg.nh)
auc_both <- pROC::auc(roc_both)

cat("\nAUCs on test_eval (A1C & FPG available):\n")
cat("  A1c+         :", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb.1),  3), "\n")
cat("  A1c/FPG+     :", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb.2),  3), "\n")
cat("  A1c only     :", round(as.numeric(auc_a1c.nh),  3), "\n")
cat("  FPG only     :", round(as.numeric(auc_fpg.nh),  3), "\n")
cat("  A1c+FPG    :", round(as.numeric(auc_both), 3), "\n")

# predicting 

# x_mat_test <- as.matrix(test_eval[, feature_cols.noFPG])
# pred_xgb   <- predict(xgb_model1, x_mat_test)

# making plot 

plot(roc_xgb.1,
     main = "ROC: XGBoost vs Logistic Baselines (NHANES)",
     lwd  = 2)
plot(roc_xgb.2, add = TRUE, lty = 5)
plot(roc_both, add = TRUE, lty = 4)
plot(roc_a1c.nh,  add = TRUE, lty = 2)
plot(roc_fpg.nh,  add = TRUE, lty = 3)

legend("bottomright",
       legend = c(
         paste0("A1c+     (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb.1),  3), ")"),
         paste0("A1c/FPG+ (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb.2),  3), ")"),
         paste0("Logistic: A1C + FPG (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_both), 3), ")"),
         paste0("Logistic: A1C only (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_a1c.nh),  3), ")"),
         paste0("Logistic: FPG only (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_fpg.nh),  3), ")")
       ),
       lty = c(1, 5, 4, 2, 3),
       bty = "n")

# this graph is only on subset of the test population that have both A1c and FPG lab data 
# which is why AUC looks different
```


```{r}
### A1c
a1c.sn.65 <- mean(roc_a1c.nh$sensitivities[roc_a1c.nh$thresholds==6.45])
a1c.sp.65 <- 1

# Call once, store the result
coords_a1c <- pROC::coords(roc_a1c.nh, "best", ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))

# Extract from the stored object
a1c.cutoff.y <- coords_a1c[["threshold"]]
a1c.sn.y     <- coords_a1c[["sensitivity"]]
a1c.sp.y     <- coords_a1c[["specificity"]]

### FPG
fpg.sn.126 <- mean(roc_fpg.nh$sensitivities[roc_fpg.nh$thresholds==125.5])
fpg.sp.126 <- 1


# Call once, store the result
coords_fpg <- pROC::coords(roc_fpg.nh, "best", ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))

# Extract from the stored object
fpg.cutoff.y <- coords_fpg[["threshold"]]
fpg.sn.y     <- coords_fpg[["sensitivity"]]
fpg.sp.y     <- coords_fpg[["specificity"]]









```



```{r NHANES ROC GGPLOT}

# Gather ROC curve data for ggplot
roc_df <- rbind(
  data.frame(
    model = "A1c+",#paste0("A1c+ (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb_eval.1), 3), ")"),
    sensitivity = roc_xgb.1$sensitivities,
    specificity = roc_xgb.1$specificities
  ),
  data.frame(
    model = "A1c/FPG+",#paste0("A1c/FPG+ (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb_eval.2), 3), ")"),
    sensitivity = roc_xgb.2$sensitivities,
    specificity = roc_xgb.2$specificities
  ),
  data.frame(
    model = "A1c+FPG",#paste0("Logistic: A1C+FPG (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_af), 3), ")"),
    sensitivity = roc_both$sensitivities,
    specificity = roc_both$specificities
  ),
  data.frame(
    model = "A1c",#paste0("A1C only (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_a1c), 3), ")"),
    sensitivity = roc_a1c.nh$sensitivities,
    specificity = roc_a1c.nh$specificities
  ),
  data.frame(
    model = "FPG",#paste0("FPG only (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_fpg), 3), ")"),
    sensitivity = roc_fpg.nh$sensitivities,
    specificity = roc_fpg.nh$specificities
  )
)

# Plot with ggplot2
ggplot() +
  geom_line(data=roc_df,aes(x = 1 - specificity, y = sensitivity, color = model, linetype = model),size = 1.1) +
  labs(
    title = "ROC: NHANES baseline diabetes (NHANES→NHANES vs logistic baselines)",
    x = "1 - Specificity (False Positive Rate)",
    y = "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)",
    color = "Model",
    linetype = "Model"
  ) + geom_point(data=data.frame(sn=fpg.sn.126,sp=1),aes(x=1-sp,y=sn),shape="square",size=4) +
  geom_point(data=data.frame(sn=a1c.sn.65,sp=1),aes(x=1-sp,y=sn),shape="circle",size=4) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Define the specific model names to match your table exactly
# Ensure these variables (auc_xgb_eval.1, etc.) exist in your environment
m1.nh    <- "A1c+"#paste0("A1c+ (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb_eval.1), 3), ")")
m2.nh    <- "A1c/FPG+"#paste0("A1c/FPG+ (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb_eval.2), 3), ")")
m_both  <- "A1c+FPG"#paste0("Logistic: A1C+FPG (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_af), 3), ")")
m_a1c.nh <- "A1c"#paste0("A1C only (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_a1c), 3), ")")
m_fpg.nh <- "FPG"#paste0("FPG only (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_fpg), 3), ")")


roc_df_thinned <- roc_df %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  # Apply a condition: if it's m1, take every 5th point; otherwise, keep all
  filter(if (cur_group()$model == m1.nh) row_number() %% 10 == 0 else TRUE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  mutate(
    # Create a row index for the specific group
    row_idx = row_number(),
    # Create a flag: keep if specificity < 1 OR if it's every 50th row when spec == 1
    keep_flag = ifelse(specificity < 1, 
                       TRUE, 
                       (row_idx %% 30 == 0 | row_idx == 1))
  ) %>%
  filter(keep_flag) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-row_idx, -keep_flag)


# 2. Build the Plot
p.roc.nh <- ggplot(roc_df_thinned , aes(x = 1 - specificity, y = sensitivity, group = model,shape=model)) +
  # Lines: A1c/FPG+ is solid, A1c+ is dashed
  geom_line(aes(linetype = model,color=model), size = 1) +
  
  # Points: Thin out the points to avoid a "blob" line
  # Using dplyr::slice explicitly to avoid the error
  geom_point(data = roc_df_thinned  %>%  filter(model %in% c(m_a1c.nh, m_fpg.nh))%>%
               dplyr::group_by(model) %>%
               dplyr::slice(seq(1, n(), by = 2)),
             aes(shape = model,color=model), size = 2) +
  # Manual Styling
  scale_linetype_manual(values = setNames(
    c("twodash", "solid", "dotted", "solid", "solid"), 
    c(m1.nh, m2.nh, m_both, m_a1c.nh, m_fpg.nh)
  )) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = setNames(
    c(NA, NA, NA, 16, 15), # 16=circle (A1c), 15=square (FPG), 17=triangle (AF)
    c(m1.nh, m2.nh, m_both, m_a1c.nh, m_fpg.nh)
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(
    c('black','grey10', 'darkgrey', 'grey', 'grey'), # 16=circle (A1c), 15=square (FPG), 17=triangle (AF)
    c(m1.nh, m2.nh, m_both, m_a1c.nh, m_fpg.nh)
  )) +  
  # scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(
  #   c('black','black', 'darkgrey', 'grey', 'grey'), # 16=circle (A1c), 15=square (FPG), 17=triangle (AF)
  #   c(m1, m2, m_af, m_a1c, m_fpg)
  # )) +  
  # Specific Markers for preselected thresholds
  # A1C 6.5 (Circle) and FPG 126 (Square)
  geom_point(data = data.frame(sn = a1c.sn.65, sp = 1), 
             aes(x = 1 - sp, y = sn),color='grey', shape = 16, size = 5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_point(data = data.frame(sn = fpg.sn.126, sp = 1), 
             aes(x = 1 - sp, y = sn),color='grey', shape = 15, size = 5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = 1, yend = 1), 
               color = "grey", linetype = "longdash", size = 0.1,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  # Labels and Greyscale Theme
  labs(
    title = "ROC: NHANES Internal Validation",
    #subtitle = "Comparing ML Models against Clinical Cutoffs (A1c 6.5%, FPG 126 mg/dL)",
    x = "1 - Specificity (False Positive Rate)",
    y = "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)",
    linetype = "Model", shape = "Model",color="Model",
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical"
  );print(p.roc.nh)
```

```{r}
# Install if you haven't yet
# install.packages("dcurves")
library(dcurves)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)


# We already have logit_af (A1C+FPG) and p_hat_af
# Let's create simple models for A1C only and FPG only to get their probabilities
logit_a1c <- glm(diabetes_flag ~ LBXGH, family = binomial, data = test_eval)
logit_fpg <- glm(diabetes_flag ~ LBXGLU, family = binomial, data = test_eval)

# Add all probability predictors to the evaluation dataframe
dca_data <- test_eval %>%
  mutate(
    p_a1c.nh = predict(logit_a1c, type = "response"),
    p_fpg.nh = predict(logit_fpg, type = "response"),
    p_af.nh  = pred_both,           # Already calculated in your code
    p_xgb.nh.1 = pred_xgb.1,            # Assuming this is already a 0-1 probability
    p_xgb.nh.2 = pred_xgb.2            # Assuming this is already a 0-1 probability
    
  )

# Calculate Net Benefit
dca_results <- dca_data %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dca(diabetes_flag ~ p_a1c.nh + p_fpg.nh + p_af.nh + p_xgb.nh.1 + p_xgb.nh.2,
      data = .,
      label = list(p_a1c.nh = "A1c only", 
                   p_fpg.nh = "FPG only", 
                   p_af.nh  = "A1c+FPG", 
                   p_xgb.nh.1 = "A1c+",
                   p_xgb.nh.2 = "A1c/FPG+"))

# Plot using ggplot2 syntax
library(ggplot2)

dca_results %>%
  plot(smooth = TRUE) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.5)) + # Often helpful to zoom in on low thresholds
  labs(title = "Decision Curve Analysis: NHANES Baseline Diabetes",
       x = "Threshold Probability",
       y = "Net Benefit") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#To align your DCA plot with your ROC plot, we need to convert the dcurves object into a tibble so we can use standard ggplot2 scales. The plot() function in dcurves is a wrapper that doesn't easily allow for the complex manual overrides (like setNames) you used in your ROC code.

#Here is the modified DCA code to ensure perfect consistency between your accuracy (ROC) and clinical utility (DCA) figures.
#Modified DCA Workflow
#R

library(dcurves)
library(tidyr)


# 1. Calculate Net Benefit for fixed thresholds
# Using the Sensitivity and Specificity we calculated earlier
# Formula: NB = Sn * Prevalence - (1 - Sp) * Prevalence * (p / (1-p))

prevalence <- mean(dca_data$diabetes_flag == 1)

# Function to calculate NB for a fixed point across all thresholds
calc_fixed_nb <- function(sn, sp, threshold_p) {
  sn * prevalence - (1 - sp) * (1 - prevalence) * (threshold_p / (1 - threshold_p))
}

# Create a data frame for these lines
threshold_range <- seq(0.01, 0.3, by = 0.01)

fixed_lines_df <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    threshold = threshold_range,
    net_benefit = calc_fixed_nb(a1c.sn.65, a1c.sp.65, threshold),
    model_label = m_a1c.nh, # Matching your legend labels
    type = "Fixed Cutoff (6.5%)"
  ),
  tibble(
    threshold = threshold_range,
    net_benefit = calc_fixed_nb(fpg.sn.126, fpg.sp.126, threshold),
    model_label = m_fpg.nh, # Matching your legend labels
    type = "Fixed Cutoff (126 mg/dL)"
  )
)

# 1. Calculate Net Benefit as you did
dca_results <- dca_data %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dca(diabetes_flag ~ p_a1c.nh + p_fpg.nh + p_af.nh + p_xgb.nh.1 + p_xgb.nh.2,
      data = .,
      label = list(p_a1c.nh = "A1c only", 
                   p_fpg.nh = "FPG only", 
                   p_af.nh  = "A1c+FPG", 
                   p_xgb.nh.1 = "A1c+",
                   p_xgb.nh.2 = "A1c/FPG+"))

# 2. Convert to a dataframe for custom ggplotting
# This allows us to use your specific m1, m2 labels and scales
dca_df <- as_tibble(dca_results) %>%
  filter(!variable %in% c("all", "none")) %>%
  mutate(model_label = case_when(
    label == "A1c+" ~ m1.nh,
    label == "A1c/FPG+" ~ m2.nh,
    label == "A1c+FPG" ~ m_both,
    label == "A1c only" ~ m_a1c.nh,
    label == "FPG only" ~ m_fpg.nh
  ))

# Separate All/None for the background lines
dca_base <- as_tibble(dca_results) %>% 
  filter(variable %in% c("all", "none"))

# 1. Update the base labels
dca_base <- dca_base %>%
  mutate(label = case_when(
    variable == "all" ~ "Test All",
    variable == "none" ~ "Test None",
    TRUE ~ label
  ))

# 2. Build the Plot with smarter label placement
p.dca.nh <- ggplot() +
  # Background Reference Lines & Labels
  geom_line(data = dca_base, aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, group = label), 
            color = "grey60", size = 0.8) +
  geom_text(data = dca_base %>% filter(variable == "none", threshold == 0.45), 
            aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, label = label),
            vjust = -1, color = "grey60", size = 3.5, fontface = "italic") +
  geom_text(data = dca_base %>% filter(variable == "all", threshold == 0.15), 
            aes(x = threshold+0.01, y = net_benefit, label = label),
            vjust = -1, color = "grey60", size = 3.5, fontface = "italic") +
  
  # FIXED THRESHOLD LINES (Now dot-dashed with Labels)
  geom_line(data = fixed_lines_df, aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, 
                                       color = model_label), 
            linetype = "dotdash", size = 0.6, alpha = 0.7) +
  
  # Add labels for A1c >= 6.5 and FPG >= 126
  # Placed at threshold 0.35 to avoid the dense 0-0.20 region
  geom_text(data = fixed_lines_df %>% 
              mutate(clean_label = ifelse(type == "Fixed Cutoff (6.5%)", "A1c \u2265 6.5", "FPG \u2265 126")) %>%
              filter(threshold == 0.03),
            aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit-0.01, label = clean_label, color = model_label),
            vjust = -1, size = 3, show.legend = FALSE) +

  # CONTINUOUS MODEL LINES (Main Curves)
  geom_line(data = dca_df, aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, 
                               color = model_label, linetype = model_label), size = 1) +
  
  # Model Points (Every 5th)
  geom_point(data = dca_df %>% group_by(model_label) %>% dplyr::slice(seq(1, n(), by = 5)),
             aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, 
                 color = model_label, shape = model_label), size = 2) +
  
  # Use existing scales
  scale_linetype_manual(values = setNames(c("twodash", "solid", "dotted", "solid", "solid"), 
                                          c(m1.nh, m2.nh, m_both, m_a1c.nh, m_fpg.nh))) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = setNames(c(NA, NA, NA, 16, 15), 
                                       c(m1.nh, m2.nh, m_both, m_a1c.nh, m_fpg.nh))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(c('black','grey10', 'darkgrey', 'grey', 'grey'), 
                                       c(m1.nh, m2.nh, m_both, m_a1c.nh, m_fpg.nh))) +
  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 0.3), ylim = c(-0.01, max(dca_df$net_benefit, na.rm=T) * 1.1)) +
  labs(title = "DCA: NHANES Internal Validation",
       x = "Threshold Probability", y = "Net Benefit",
       color = "Model", shape = "Model", linetype = "Model") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.box = "vertical", legend.key.width = unit(3, "line"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()); print(p.dca.nh)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(pROC)


get_roc_stats <- function(roc_obj, model_name) {
  # 1. Get the best coordinates
  best_coords <- pROC::coords(roc_obj, "best", ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"), transpose = TRUE)

  # 3. Format into a clean row
  tibble(
    Model = model_name,
    AUC = round(as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_obj)),2),
    Threshold = best_coords["threshold"],
    # Format as "Value (95% CI: Low - High)"
    Sensitivity = paste0(round(best_coords["sensitivity"], 2)),
    Specificity = paste0(round(best_coords["specificity"], 2))
  )
}


get_roc_stats_with_ci <- function(roc_obj, model_name) {
  # 1. Get the best coordinates
  best_coords <- pROC::coords(roc_obj, "best", ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"), transpose = TRUE)
  
  # 2. Calculate Confidence Intervals for Sn and Sp at that best threshold
  # Note: boot.n = 2000 is standard for publication
  cis <- pROC::ci.coords(roc_obj, x = best_coords["threshold"], 
                         input = "threshold", 
                         ret = c("sensitivity", "specificity"),
                         boot.n = 100)
  
  # 3. Format into a clean row
  tibble(
    Model = model_name,
    AUC = round(as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_obj)),2),
    Threshold = best_coords["threshold"],
    # Format as "Value (95% CI: Low - High)"
    Sensitivity = paste0(round(best_coords["sensitivity"], 2), " (", 
                         round(cis$sensitivity[1], 2), "-", 
                         round(cis$sensitivity[3], 2), ")"),
    Specificity = paste0(round(best_coords["specificity"], 2), " (", 
                         round(cis$specificity[1], 2), "-", 
                         round(cis$specificity[3], 2), ")")
  )
}

roc_list <- list(
  "A1c+ (NHANES→NHANES)" = roc_xgb.1,
  "A1c/FPG+ (NHANES→NHANES)" = roc_xgb.2,
  "A1C + FPG (Logistic)"    = roc_both,
  "A1C Only"               = roc_a1c.nh,
  "FPG Only"               = roc_fpg.nh
)


get_fixed_stats <- function(roc_obj, model_name, threshold) {
  # Get Sn and Sp at exactly your threshold
  coords <- pROC::coords(roc_obj, x = threshold, input = "threshold", 
                         ret = c("sensitivity", "specificity"), transpose = TRUE)
  
 
  
  tibble(
    Model = model_name,
    AUC = round(as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_obj)),2),
    Threshold = threshold,
    Sensitivity = paste0(round(coords["sensitivity"], 2)),
    Specificity = paste0(round(coords["specificity"], 2)),
    Type = "Preselected"
  )
}
# Helper for manual/fixed thresholds (like A1C = 6.5)
get_fixed_stats_with_ci <- function(roc_obj, model_name, threshold) {
  # Get Sn and Sp at exactly your threshold
  coords <- pROC::coords(roc_obj, x = threshold, input = "threshold", 
                         ret = c("sensitivity", "specificity"), transpose = TRUE)
  
  # Bootstrapped CIs
  cis <- pROC::ci.coords(roc_obj, x = threshold, input = "threshold", 
                         ret = c("sensitivity", "specificity"), boot.n = 100)
  
  tibble(
    Model = model_name,
    AUC = round(as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_obj)),2),
    Threshold = threshold,
    Sensitivity = paste0(round(coords["sensitivity"], 2), " (", 
                         round(cis$sensitivity[1], 2), "-", round(cis$sensitivity[3], 2), ")"),
    Specificity = paste0(round(coords["specificity"], 2), " (", 
                         round(cis$specificity[1], 2), "-", round(cis$specificity[3], 2), ")"),
    Type = "Preselected"
  )
}

# 1. Run stats for XGBoost (Optimal)
tab_xgb.1 <- get_roc_stats(roc_xgb.1, "A1c+") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_xgb.2 <- get_roc_stats(roc_xgb.2, "A1c/FPG+") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_af <- get_roc_stats(roc_both, "A1c+FPG") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_a1c <- get_roc_stats(roc_a1c.nh, "A1c") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_fpg <- get_roc_stats(roc_fpg.nh, "FPG") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")


# 2. Run stats for your preselected lab values

tab_a1c.fixed <- get_fixed_stats(roc_a1c.nh, "A1C \u2265 6.5", threshold = 6.45)
tab_a1c.fixed$AUC <- NA
tab_fpg.fixed <- get_fixed_stats(roc_fpg.nh, "FPG \u2265 126", threshold = 125.5)
tab_fpg.fixed$AUC <- NA


# Combine
final_comparison_table.noCI.nh <- bind_rows(tab_xgb.2, tab_af, tab_fpg, tab_fpg.fixed, tab_xgb.1,tab_a1c, tab_a1c.fixed)
print(final_comparison_table.noCI.nh)

# 1. Run stats for XGBoost (Optimal)
tab_xgb.1 <- get_roc_stats_with_ci(roc_xgb.1, "A1c+") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_xgb.2 <- get_roc_stats_with_ci(roc_xgb.2, "A1c/FPG+") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_af <- get_roc_stats_with_ci(roc_both, "A1c+FPG") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_a1c <- get_roc_stats_with_ci(roc_a1c.nh, "A1c") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_fpg <- get_roc_stats_with_ci(roc_fpg.nh, "FPG") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")


# 2. Run stats for your preselected lab values
tab_a1c.fixed <- get_fixed_stats_with_ci(roc_a1c.nh, "A1C \u2265 6.5", threshold = 6.45)
tab_a1c.fixed$AUC <- NA
tab_fpg.fixed <- get_fixed_stats_with_ci(roc_fpg.nh, "FPG \u2265 126", threshold = 125.5)
tab_fpg.fixed$AUC <- NA

# Combine
final_comparison_table.nh <- bind_rows(tab_xgb.2, tab_af, tab_fpg, tab_fpg.fixed, tab_xgb.1,tab_a1c, tab_a1c.fixed)
print(final_comparison_table.nh)
```






```{r LOAD DPPOS}
# load DPPOS here and apply the preprocessing (impute and encode)

library(pROC)


df_dppos_model <- readRDS("~/Documents/Data/DPPOS_V6/New/df_dppos_model_full.rds")
cat("Rows in raw DPPOS model df:", nrow(df_dppos_model), "\n")


# can change this to df_dppos_aligned <- test_dppos_raw
# if we want to only look at the DPPOS test set for calculating ROC 

### Add empty cols
diff.cols <- setdiff(feature_cols.noFPG,names(df_dppos_model))
new.cols<-diff.cols[!diff.cols %in% c("Year", "has_a1c", "has_fpg", "has_ogt", "meets_a1c", 
"meets_fpg", "meets_ogt","BMI")]

df_dppos_model[new.cols]<- NA


df_dppos_aligned <- df_dppos_model

# cat_cols <- intersect(c("RIAGENDR","RIDRETH1"), feature_cols.noFPG)
# if (length(cat_cols) > 0) {
#   df_dppos_aligned <- encode_apply(df_dppos_aligned, enc)
# }

#df_dppos_aligned <- impute_apply(df_dppos_aligned, imp)
saveRDS(object = df_dppos_aligned,file="~/Documents/NHANES-DMDx/df_dppos_aligned.rds")
```


```{r DPPOS FILTER A1c}
#df_dppos_aligned <- df_dppos_aligned %>% filter(LBXGH<6.5, LBXGLU <126)
```

```{r}
# df_dppos_aligned %>% summarise(two =sum(LBXGLT<200,na.rm=TRUE))
# 
# df_dppos_aligned %>% ggplot() + geom_point(aes(x=LBXGH,y=LBXGLU,color=LBXGLT))
```


```{r}



# predict on DPPOS using NHANES-trained XGBoost

x_mat_dppos.1     <- as.matrix(df_dppos_aligned[, feature_cols.noFPG])
pred_prob_dppos.1 <- predict(xgb_model1, x_mat_dppos.1)
df_dppos_aligned$pred_xgb.1 <- pred_prob_dppos.1


x_mat_dppos.2    <- as.matrix(df_dppos_aligned[, feature_cols])
pred_prob_dppos.2 <- predict(xgb_model2, x_mat_dppos.2)
df_dppos_aligned$pred_xgb.2 <- pred_prob_dppos.2

```



```{r}

#  DPPOS baseline lab-defined diabetes label (same rule as NHANES)

df_dppos_aligned <- df_dppos_aligned %>%
  mutate(
    has_a1c = !is.na(LBXGH),
    has_fpg = !is.na(LBXGLU),
    has_ogt = !is.na(LBXGLT),

    meets_a1c = has_a1c & LBXGH  >= 6.5,
    meets_fpg = has_fpg & LBXGLU >= 126,
    meets_ogt = has_ogt & LBXGLT >= 200,

    any_obs  = has_a1c | has_fpg | has_ogt,
    any_meet = meets_a1c | meets_fpg | meets_ogt,

    diabetes_flag_bl = case_when(
      any_meet            ~ 1,
      any_obs & !any_meet ~ 0,
      TRUE                ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

has_bl <- !is.na(df_dppos_aligned$diabetes_flag_bl)
cat("DPPOS rows with baseline label:", sum(has_bl), "of", nrow(df_dppos_aligned), "\n")

```

```{r}
# Evaluate DPPOS using NHANES training predictions (XGBoost)

roc_dppos_bl_xgb.1 <- pROC::roc(
  response  = df_dppos_aligned$diabetes_flag_bl[has_bl],
  predictor = df_dppos_aligned$pred_xgb.1[has_bl],
  quiet     = TRUE
)

auc_dppos_bl_xgb.1 <- pROC::auc(roc_dppos_bl_xgb.1)
cat("A1c+ NHANES→DPPOS AUC     =", round(as.numeric(auc_dppos_bl_xgb.1), 3), "\n")


roc_dppos_bl_xgb.2 <- pROC::roc(
  response  = df_dppos_aligned$diabetes_flag_bl[has_bl],
  predictor = df_dppos_aligned$pred_xgb.2[has_bl],
  quiet     = TRUE
)

auc_dppos_bl_xgb.2 <- pROC::auc(roc_dppos_bl_xgb.2)
cat("A1c/FPG+ NHANES→DPPOS AUC =", round(as.numeric(auc_dppos_bl_xgb.2), 3), "\n")

```

```{r}
# Baseline benchmarks on same labeled DPPOS rows + ROC curves
dppos_bl_eval <- df_dppos_aligned[has_bl, ]

# Restrict to only people with both labs 
dppos_bl_eval <- dppos_bl_eval %>% filter(has_a1c , has_fpg) ### HERE

# A1C 
just_a1c <- dppos_bl_eval$LBXGH
p_hat_a1c <- just_a1c
roc_a1c   <- pROC::roc(dppos_bl_eval$diabetes_flag_bl, p_hat_a1c, quiet = TRUE)
auc_a1c   <- pROC::auc(roc_a1c)


# FPG 
just_fpg <- dppos_bl_eval$LBXGLU
p_hat_fpg <- just_fpg
roc_fpg   <- pROC::roc(dppos_bl_eval$diabetes_flag_bl, p_hat_fpg, quiet = TRUE)
auc_fpg   <- pROC::auc(roc_fpg)

# A1C + FPG logistic regression 
logit_af <- glm_both#glm(diabetes_flag ~ LBXGH + LBXGLU, family = binomial, data = train_eval) #train_eval
pred_hat_af  <- predict(logit_af, newdata = dppos_bl_eval, type = "response")

p_hat_af <- pred_hat_af
roc_af   <- pROC::roc(dppos_bl_eval$diabetes_flag_bl, pred_hat_af, quiet = TRUE)
auc_af   <- pROC::auc(roc_af)

# A1c+ XGBoost ROC
roc_xgb_eval.1 <- pROC::roc(
  response  = dppos_bl_eval$diabetes_flag_bl,
  predictor = dppos_bl_eval$pred_xgb.1,
  quiet     = TRUE
)
auc_xgb_eval.1 <- pROC::auc(roc_xgb_eval.1)



# A1c/FPG+ XGBoost ROC
roc_xgb_eval.2 <- pROC::roc(
  response  = dppos_bl_eval$diabetes_flag_bl,
  predictor = dppos_bl_eval$pred_xgb.2,
  quiet     = TRUE
)
auc_xgb_eval.2 <- pROC::auc(roc_xgb_eval.2)

cat("DPPOS baseline AUCs (same eval rows):\n")
cat("  A1c+ (NHANES→DPPOS):", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb_eval.1), 3), "\n")
cat("  A1c/FPG+ (NHANES→DPPOS):", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb_eval.2), 3), "\n")
cat("  Logistic: A1C only     :", round(as.numeric(auc_a1c), 3), "\n")
cat("  Logistic: FPG only     :", round(as.numeric(auc_fpg), 3), "\n")
cat("  Logistic: (NHANES→DPPOS) A1C + FPG    :", round(as.numeric(auc_af), 3), "\n")

# Plot all ROC curves
plot(
  roc_xgb_eval.1,
  main = "ROC: DPPOS baseline diabetes (NHANES→DPPOS vs logistic baselines)",
  lwd  = 2, ,xlim=c(1,0)
)
plot(roc_xgb_eval.2,  add = TRUE, lty = 5,,xlim=c(1,0))
plot(roc_af,  add = TRUE, lty = 4,,xlim=c(1,0))
plot(roc_a1c, add = TRUE, lty = 2,,xlim=c(1,0))
plot(roc_fpg, add = TRUE, lty = 3,xlim=c(1,0))

legend(
  "bottomright",
  legend = c(
    paste0("A1c/FPG+ (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb_eval.2), 3), ")"),
    paste0("Logistic: A1C+FPG (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_af), 3), ")"),
    paste0("Logistic: FPG only (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_fpg), 3), ")"),
    paste0("A1c+ (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb_eval.1), 3), ")"),    
    paste0("Logistic: A1C only (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_a1c), 3), ")")    
  ),
  lty = c(1, 5, 4, 2, 3),
  bty = "n"
)


```
```{r}
saveRDS(object = roc_xgb_eval.2,file="~/Dropbox/NHANES manuscript/rox_xgb_eval.2.rds")
summary(roc_xgb_eval.2)

length(roc_xgb_eval.2$sensitivities)
```

```{r}
### A1c
a1c.sn.65 <- mean(roc_a1c$sensitivities[roc_a1c$thresholds==6.45])
a1c.sp.65 <- 1

# Call once, store the result
coords_a1c <- pROC::coords(roc_a1c, "best", ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))

# Extract from the stored object
a1c.cutoff.y <- coords_a1c[["threshold"]]
a1c.sn.y     <- coords_a1c[["sensitivity"]]
a1c.sp.y     <- coords_a1c[["specificity"]]

### FPG
fpg.sn.126 <- mean(roc_fpg$sensitivities[roc_fpg$thresholds==125.5])
fpg.sp.126 <- 1


# Call once, store the result
coords_fpg <- pROC::coords(roc_fpg, "best", ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))

# Extract from the stored object
fpg.cutoff.y <- coords_fpg[["threshold"]]
fpg.sn.y     <- coords_fpg[["sensitivity"]]
fpg.sp.y     <- coords_fpg[["specificity"]]








```



```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(pROC)




roc_list <- list(
  "A1c+ (NHANES→DPPOS)" = roc_xgb_eval.1,
  "A1c/FPG+ (NHANES→DPPOS)" = roc_xgb_eval.2,
  "A1C + FPG (Logistic)"    = roc_af,
  "A1C Only"               = roc_a1c,
  "FPG Only"               = roc_fpg
)
#######



# 1. Run stats for XGBoost (Optimal)
tab_xgb.1 <- get_roc_stats(roc_xgb_eval.1, "A1c+") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_xgb.2 <- get_roc_stats(roc_xgb_eval.2, "A1c/FPG+") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_af <- get_roc_stats(roc_af, "A1c+FPG") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_a1c <- get_roc_stats(roc_a1c, "A1c") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_fpg <- get_roc_stats(roc_fpg, "FPG") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")


# 2. Run stats for your preselected lab values
tab_a1c.fixed <- get_fixed_stats(roc_a1c, "A1C \u2265 6.5", threshold = 6.45)
tab_a1c.fixed$AUC <- NA
tab_fpg.fixed <- get_fixed_stats(roc_fpg, "FPG \u2265 126", threshold = 125.5)
tab_fpg.fixed$AUC <- NA

# Combine
final_comparison_table.noCI.dp <- bind_rows(tab_xgb.2, tab_af, tab_fpg, tab_fpg.fixed, tab_xgb.1,tab_a1c, tab_a1c.fixed)
print(final_comparison_table.noCI.dp)

# 1. Run stats for XGBoost (Optimal)
tab_xgb.1 <- get_roc_stats_with_ci(roc_xgb_eval.1, "A1c+") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_xgb.2 <- get_roc_stats_with_ci(roc_xgb_eval.2, "A1c/FPG+") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_af <- get_roc_stats_with_ci(roc_af, "A1c+FPG") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_a1c <- get_roc_stats_with_ci(roc_a1c, "A1c") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")
tab_fpg <- get_roc_stats_with_ci(roc_fpg, "FPG") %>% mutate(Type = "Optimal")


# 2. Run stats for your preselected lab values
tab_a1c.fixed <- get_fixed_stats_with_ci(roc_a1c, "A1C \u2265 6.5", threshold = 6.45)
tab_a1c.fixed$AUC <- NA
tab_fpg.fixed <- get_fixed_stats_with_ci(roc_fpg, "FPG \u2265 126", threshold = 125.5)
tab_fpg.fixed$AUC <- NA

# Combine
final_comparison_table.dp <- bind_rows(tab_xgb.2, tab_af, tab_fpg, tab_fpg.fixed, tab_xgb.1,tab_a1c, tab_a1c.fixed)
print(final_comparison_table.dp)


```







```{r}
# Gather ROC curve data for ggplot
roc_df <- rbind(
  data.frame(
    model = "A1c+",#paste0("A1c+ (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb_eval.1), 3), ")"),
    sensitivity = roc_xgb_eval.1$sensitivities,
    specificity = roc_xgb_eval.1$specificities
  ),
  data.frame(
    model = "A1c/FPG+",#paste0("A1c/FPG+ (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb_eval.2), 3), ")"),
    sensitivity = roc_xgb_eval.2$sensitivities,
    specificity = roc_xgb_eval.2$specificities
  ),
  data.frame(
    model = "A1c+FPG",#paste0("Logistic: A1C+FPG (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_af), 3), ")"),
    sensitivity = roc_af$sensitivities,
    specificity = roc_af$specificities
  ),
  data.frame(
    model = "A1c",#paste0("A1C only (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_a1c), 3), ")"),
    sensitivity = roc_a1c$sensitivities,
    specificity = roc_a1c$specificities
  ),
  data.frame(
    model = "FPG",#paste0("FPG only (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_fpg), 3), ")"),
    sensitivity = roc_fpg$sensitivities,
    specificity = roc_fpg$specificities
  )
)

# Plot with ggplot2
ggplot() +
  geom_line(data=roc_df %>% 
              mutate(model = factor(model,
                                       levels=c("A1c/FPG+","A1c+FPG","FPG","A1c+","A1c"))),aes(x = 1 - specificity, y = sensitivity, color = model),size = 1.1) +
  labs(
    title = "External Validation",
    x = "1 - Specificity (False Positive Rate)",
    y = "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)",
    color = "Model",
    linetype = "Model"
  ) + geom_point(data=data.frame(sn=fpg.sn.126,sp=1),aes(x=1-sp,y=sn),color="purple",shape="square",size=4) +
  geom_point(data=data.frame(sn=a1c.sn.65,sp=1),aes(x=1-sp,y=sn),color="darkgreen",shape="circle",size=4) +
  scale_color_manual(values=
                       c("A1c" = "red", 
                         "A1c/FPG+" = "blue", 
                         "A1c+" = "darkgreen", 
                         "A1c+FPG" = "orange",
                         "FPG" = "purple")) + 
  theme_minimal() + theme.1
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Define the specific model names to match your table exactly
# Ensure these variables (auc_xgb_eval.1, etc.) exist in your environment
m1    <- "A1c+"#paste0("A1c+ (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb_eval.1), 3), ")")
m2    <- "A1c/FPG+"#paste0("A1c/FPG+ (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_xgb_eval.2), 3), ")")
m_af  <- "A1c+FPG"#paste0("Logistic: A1C+FPG (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_af), 3), ")")
m_a1c <- "A1c"#paste0("A1C only (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_a1c), 3), ")")
m_fpg <- "FPG"#paste0("FPG only (AUC=", round(as.numeric(auc_fpg), 3), ")")


roc_df_thinned <- roc_df %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  # Apply a condition: if it's m1, take every 5th point; otherwise, keep all
  filter(if (cur_group()$model == m1) row_number() %% 10 == 0 else TRUE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(model) %>%
  mutate(
    # Create a row index for the specific group
    row_idx = row_number(),
    # Create a flag: keep if specificity < 1 OR if it's every 50th row when spec == 1
    keep_flag = ifelse(specificity < 1, 
                       TRUE, 
                       (row_idx %% 20 == 0 | row_idx ==1))
  ) %>%
  filter(keep_flag) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-row_idx, -keep_flag)

# 2. Build the Plot
p.roc.dp <- ggplot(roc_df_thinned, aes(x = 1 - specificity, y = sensitivity, group = model,shape=model)) +
  # Lines: A1c/FPG+ is solid, A1c+ is dashed
  geom_line(aes(linetype = model,color=model), size = 1) +
  
  # Points: Thin out the points to avoid a "blob" line
  # Using dplyr::slice explicitly to avoid the error
  geom_point(data = roc_df_thinned,
             aes(shape = model,color=model), size = 2) +
  # Manual Styling
  scale_linetype_manual(values = setNames(
    c("twodash", "solid", "dotted", "solid", "solid"), 
    c(m1, m2, m_af, m_a1c, m_fpg)
  )) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = setNames(
    c(NA, NA, NA, 16, 15), # 16=circle (A1c), 15=square (FPG), 17=triangle (AF)
    c(m1, m2, m_af, m_a1c, m_fpg)
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(
    c('black','grey10', 'darkgrey', 'grey', 'grey'), # 16=circle (A1c), 15=square (FPG), 17=triangle (AF)
    c(m1, m2, m_af, m_a1c, m_fpg)
  )) +  
  # scale_fill_manual(values = setNames(
  #   c('black','black', 'darkgrey', 'grey', 'grey'), # 16=circle (A1c), 15=square (FPG), 17=triangle (AF)
  #   c(m1, m2, m_af, m_a1c, m_fpg)
  # )) +  
  # Specific Markers for preselected thresholds
  # A1C 6.5 (Circle) and FPG 126 (Square)
  geom_point(data = data.frame(sn = a1c.sn.65, sp = 1), 
             aes(x = 1 - sp, y = sn),color='grey', shape = 16, size = 5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_point(data = data.frame(sn = fpg.sn.126, sp = 1), 
             aes(x = 1 - sp, y = sn),color='grey', shape = 15, size = 5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = 1, yend = 1), 
               color = "grey", linetype = "longdash", size = 0.1,
               inherit.aes = FALSE) +  
  # Labels and Greyscale Theme
  labs(
    title = "ROC: DPPOS External Validation",
    #subtitle = "Comparing ML Models against Clinical Cutoffs (A1c 6.5%, FPG 126 mg/dL)",
    x = "1 - Specificity (False Positive Rate)",
    y = "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)",
    linetype = "Model", shape = "Model",color="Model",
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical"
  );print(p.roc.dp)
```


```{r}
roc_df_thinned %>% 
  filter(model == "A1c/FPG+") %>%
  count()
```



```{r}
# Install if you haven't yet
# install.packages("dcurves")
library(dcurves)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)


# We already have logit_af (A1C+FPG) and p_hat_af
# Let's create simple models for A1C only and FPG only to get their probabilities
logit_a1c <- glm(diabetes_flag_bl ~ LBXGH, family = binomial, data = dppos_bl_eval)
logit_fpg <- glm(diabetes_flag_bl ~ LBXGLU, family = binomial, data = dppos_bl_eval)

# Add all probability predictors to the evaluation dataframe
dca_data <- dppos_bl_eval %>%
  mutate(
    p_a1c = predict(logit_a1c, type = "response"),
    p_fpg = predict(logit_fpg, type = "response"),
    p_af  = p_hat_af,           # Already calculated in your code
    p_xgb.1 = pred_xgb.1,            # Assuming this is already a 0-1 probability
    p_xgb.2 = pred_xgb.2            # Assuming this is already a 0-1 probability
    
  )

# Calculate Net Benefit
dca_results <- dca_data %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dca(diabetes_flag_bl ~ p_a1c + p_fpg + p_af + p_xgb.1 + p_xgb.2,
      data = .,
      label = list(p_a1c = "A1C only", 
                   p_fpg = "FPG only", 
                   p_af  = "A1C + FPG", 
                   p_xgb.1 = "A1c+",
                   p_xgb.2 = "A1c/FPG+"))

# Plot using ggplot2 syntax
library(ggplot2)

dca_results %>%
  plot(smooth = TRUE) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.5)) + # Often helpful to zoom in on low thresholds
  labs(title = "Decision Curve Analysis: DPPOS Baseline Diabetes",
       x = "Threshold Probability",
       y = "Net Benefit") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
#To align your DCA plot with your ROC plot, we need to convert the dcurves object into a tibble so we can use standard ggplot2 scales. The plot() function in dcurves is a wrapper that doesn't easily allow for the complex manual overrides (like setNames) you used in your ROC code.

#Here is the modified DCA code to ensure perfect consistency between your accuracy (ROC) and clinical utility (DCA) figures.
#Modified DCA Workflow
#R

library(dcurves)
library(tidyr)


# 1. Calculate Net Benefit for fixed thresholds
# Using the Sensitivity and Specificity we calculated earlier
# Formula: NB = Sn * Prevalence - (1 - Sp) * Prevalence * (p / (1-p))

prevalence <- mean(dca_data$diabetes_flag_bl == 1)

# Function to calculate NB for a fixed point across all thresholds
calc_fixed_nb <- function(sn, sp, threshold_p) {
  sn * prevalence - (1 - sp) * (1 - prevalence) * (threshold_p / (1 - threshold_p))
}

# Create a data frame for these lines
threshold_range <- seq(0.01, 0.3, by = 0.01)

fixed_lines_df <- bind_rows(
  tibble(
    threshold = threshold_range,
    net_benefit = calc_fixed_nb(a1c.sn.65, a1c.sp.65, threshold),
    model_label = m_a1c, # Matching your legend labels
    type = "Fixed Cutoff (6.5%)"
  ),
  tibble(
    threshold = threshold_range,
    net_benefit = calc_fixed_nb(fpg.sn.126, fpg.sp.126, threshold),
    model_label = m_fpg, # Matching your legend labels
    type = "Fixed Cutoff (126 mg/dL)"
  )
)

# 1. Calculate Net Benefit as you did
dca_results <- dca_data %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dca(diabetes_flag_bl ~ p_a1c + p_fpg + p_af + p_xgb.1 + p_xgb.2,
      data = .,
      label = list(p_a1c = "A1C only", 
                   p_fpg = "FPG only", 
                   p_af  = "A1C + FPG", 
                   p_xgb.1 = "A1c+",
                   p_xgb.2 = "A1c/FPG+"))

# 2. Convert to a dataframe for custom ggplotting
# This allows us to use your specific m1, m2 labels and scales
dca_df <- as_tibble(dca_results) %>%
  filter(!variable %in% c("all", "none")) %>%
  mutate(model_label = case_when(
    label == "A1c+" ~ m1,
    label == "A1c/FPG+" ~ m2,
    label == "A1C + FPG" ~ m_af,
    label == "A1C only" ~ m_a1c,
    label == "FPG only" ~ m_fpg
  ))

# Separate All/None for the background lines
dca_base <- as_tibble(dca_results) %>% 
  filter(variable %in% c("all", "none"))

# 1. Update the base labels
dca_base <- dca_base %>%
  mutate(label = case_when(
    variable == "all" ~ "Test All",
    variable == "none" ~ "Test None",
    TRUE ~ label
  ))

# 2. Build the Plot with smarter label placement
p.dca.dp <- ggplot() +
  # Background Reference Lines & Labels
  geom_line(data = dca_base, aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, group = label), 
            color = "grey60", size = 0.8) +
  geom_text(data = dca_base %>% filter(variable == "none", threshold == 0.25), 
            aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, label = label),
            vjust = -1, color = "grey60", size = 3.5, fontface = "italic") +
  geom_text(data = dca_base %>% filter(variable == "all", threshold == 0.20), 
            aes(x = threshold+0.01, y = net_benefit, label = label),
            vjust = -1, color = "grey60", size = 3.5, fontface = "italic") +
  
  # FIXED THRESHOLD LINES (Now dot-dashed with Labels)
  geom_line(data = fixed_lines_df, aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, 
                                       color = model_label), 
            linetype = "dotdash", size = 0.6, alpha = 0.7) +
  
  # Add labels for A1c >= 6.5 and FPG >= 126
  # Placed at threshold 0.35 to avoid the dense 0-0.20 region
  geom_text(data = fixed_lines_df %>% 
              mutate(clean_label = ifelse(type == "Fixed Cutoff (6.5%)", "A1c \u2265 6.5", "FPG \u2265 126")) %>%
              filter(threshold == 0.03),
            aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit-0.02, label = clean_label, color = model_label),
            vjust = -1, size = 3, show.legend = FALSE) +

  # CONTINUOUS MODEL LINES (Main Curves)
  geom_line(data = dca_df, aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, 
                               color = model_label, linetype = model_label), size = 1) +
  
  # Model Points (Every 5th)
  geom_point(data = dca_df %>% group_by(model_label) %>% dplyr::slice(seq(1, n(), by = 5)),
             aes(x = threshold, y = net_benefit, 
                 color = model_label, shape = model_label), size = 2) +
  
  # Use existing scales
  scale_linetype_manual(values = setNames(c("twodash", "solid", "dotted", "solid", "solid"), 
                                          c(m1, m2, m_af, m_a1c, m_fpg))) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = setNames(c(NA, NA, NA, 16, 15), 
                                       c(m1, m2, m_af, m_a1c, m_fpg))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = setNames(c('black','grey10', 'darkgrey', 'grey', 'grey'), 
                                       c(m1, m2, m_af, m_a1c, m_fpg))) +
  
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 0.3), ylim = c(-0.01, max(dca_df$net_benefit, na.rm=T) * 1.1)) +
  labs(title = "DCA: DPPOS External Validation",
       x = "Threshold Probability", y = "Net Benefit",
       color = "Model", shape = "Model", linetype = "Model") +
  theme_minimal() + 
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.box = "vertical", legend.key.width = unit(3, "line"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank());print(p.dca.dp)
```




```{r}
library(patchwork)



# Define common scales to ensure perfect merging
# 1. Force the plots to have the same scales (crucial for collection)
common_scales.nh <- list(
  scale_color_manual(name = "Model", values = setNames(c('black','grey10', 'darkgrey', 'grey', 'grey'), c(m1.nh, m2.nh, m_both, m_a1c.nh, m_fpg.nh))),
  scale_linetype_manual(name = "Model", values = setNames(c("twodash", "solid", "dotted", "solid", "solid"), c(m1.nh, m2.nh, m_both, m_a1c.nh, m_fpg.nh))),
  scale_shape_manual(name = "Model", values = setNames(c(NA, NA, NA, 16, 15), c(m1.nh, m2.nh, m_both, m_a1c.nh, m_fpg.nh)))
)

common_scales.dp <- list(
  scale_color_manual(name = "Model", values = setNames(c('black','grey10', 'darkgrey', 'grey', 'grey'), c(m1, m2, m_af, m_a1c ,m_fpg))),
  scale_linetype_manual(name = "Model", values = setNames(c("twodash", "solid", "dotted", "solid", "solid"), c(m1, m2, m_af, m_a1c ,m_fpg))),
  scale_shape_manual(name = "Model", values = setNames(c(NA, NA, NA, 16, 15), c(m1, m2, m_af, m_a1c ,m_fpg)))
)

# Apply scales to objects
pa <- p.roc.nh + common_scales.nh
pb <- p.dca.nh + common_scales.nh
pc <- p.roc.dp + common_scales.dp
pd <- p.dca.dp + common_scales.dp

# 2. Design the Layout
# We create a 3-row layout: 
# Row 1: A | B
# Row 2: C | D
# Row 3: The Legend (guide_area)
final_layout <- (pa + pb) / (pc + pd) / guide_area() + 
  plot_layout(
    guides = 'collect', 
    heights = c(1, 1, 0.3) # Makes the legend row shorter than the plots
  ) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A') & 
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.key.width = unit(3, "line"),
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) & theme(legend.direction = "horizontal", legend.box = "horizontal")

# Display the result
final_layout

```
```{r}
library(cowplot)
# Extract legend from the NHANES plot
shared_legend <- get_legend(pa + theme(legend.position = "bottom"))

# Create the grid without legends
main_grid <- plot_grid(pa + theme(legend.position="none"), 
                       pb + theme(legend.position="none"), 
                       pc + theme(legend.position="none"), 
                       pd + theme(legend.position="none"), 
                       ncol = 2, labels = "AUTO")

# Add the legend back at the bottom
final_plot <- plot_grid(main_grid, shared_legend, ncol = 1, rel_heights = c(1, 0.2))
```



```{r}
# Yes, embedding tables directly into the plot area (the "insets" approach) is a common way to save vertical space in high-impact journals. It makes the figure feel more integrated and avoids the "wall of text" look below the graphs.
# 
# To do this, we use annotation_custom(). This allows us to treat your tableGrob as a layer within the plot itself.
# 1. Update the Plot Objects with Embedded Tables
# 
# We will place the tables in the bottom-right corner of the ROC plots (A and C). Since ROC plots are usually "empty" in that bottom-right quadrant (low sensitivity, high specificity), it’s the perfect spot.
# R

library(grid)
library(gridExtra)
# 1. Update theme slightly for legibility (0.2mm is too tight for print)
inset_table_theme <- ttheme_default(
  base_size = 8,
  core = list(
    bg_params = list(fill = "white", col = "white"), 
    fg_params = list(hjust = 0, x = 0.1),
    padding.x = unit(1, "mm"), 
    padding.y = unit(1, "mm") 
  ),
  colhead = list(
    bg_params = list(fill = "white", col = "white"),
    fg_params = list(fontface = "bold", hjust = 0, x = 0.1),
    padding.x = unit(1, "mm"),
    padding.y = unit(1, "mm")
  )
)

# Function to create the table grob
make_inset_table <- function(df) {
  tableGrob(df %>% select(Model, AUC, Sensitivity, Specificity), 
            rows = NULL, theme = inset_table_theme)
}

# 2. Re-create and Insert with tighter coordinates
table_a <- make_inset_table(final_comparison_table.noCI.nh)
table_c <- make_inset_table(final_comparison_table.noCI.dp)

p_a_with_table <- pa +  
  annotation_custom(table_a, xmin = 0.32, xmax = 0.9, ymin = 0.05, ymax = 0.62)

p_c_with_table <- pc +  
  annotation_custom(table_c, xmin = 0.32, xmax = 0.9, ymin = 0.05, ymax = 0.62)


library(cowplot)


# Arrange the 4 panels
main_grid <- plot_grid(
  p_a_with_table + theme(legend.position = "none"),
  pb + theme(legend.position = "none"),
  p_c_with_table + theme(legend.position = "none"),
  pd + theme(legend.position = "none"),
  ncol = 2, nrow = 2,
  labels = "AUTO", label_size = 14, label_fontface = "bold",
  align = 'vh'
)

# Update the shared legend before the final save
shared_legend <- get_legend(
  p_a_with_table + 
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.width = unit(3.5, "line"),
    # NEW: White background styling
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "white"), 
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6) # Adds a little internal padding
  ) +
  guides(
    color = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE),
    linetype = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE),
    shape = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)
  )
)

# Re-assemble one last time
final_figure <- plot_grid(
  main_grid, 
  shared_legend, 
  ncol = 1, 
  rel_heights = c(1, 0.07) # Increased to 0.2 for the 2-row legend
) ;print(final_figure)
```


```{r}
# Adjusted height to 9.5in to accommodate the larger legend and tables
ggsave("~/Dropbox/NHANES manuscript/Figure1_Final_Sub.tiff", 
       plot = final_figure, 
       width = 7, 
       height = 9.5, 
       units = "in", 
       dpi = 300, 
       scale = 1,
       compression = "lzw")
```


```{r}
dppos_bl_eval %>% select(diabetes_flag_bl,meets_fpg) %>% filter(meets_fpg==TRUE,diabetes_flag_bl==1) %>% nrow() / dppos_bl_eval %>% filter(diabetes_flag_bl==1) %>% nrow()


```


```{r Shap Build A1c+}

contr.full <- predict(xgb_model1, dtest.1, predcontrib = TRUE)
shap.dat.full<-xgb.plot.shap(as.matrix(test_model1[,feature_cols.noFPG]), contr.full, model = xgb_model1, top_n = 20, n_col = 5)

theme.1 <- theme(axis.text=element_text(size=8),
            axis.title=element_text(size=12),
            plot.tag.position = c(0.01,0.99),
            plot.tag = element_text(size=20),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5,size=18)) 

feature_cols
renamed_labels <- c( 'LBXGLU' = 'FPG',
                                      'LBXGH' = 'A1c',
                                      'BPXSY' = 'Systolic BP',

                                      'BMXWAIST' = 'Waist Circ.',
 
                                      'BPXDI' = 'Diastolic BP',
   
                                      'LBXSCR' = 'Creatinine',
  
                     'DMDHHSIZ' = '# in Household',
                     'RIDRETH1' = "Race/Ethnicity",
                     'RIAGENDR' = "Gender",
                     "INDFMPIR" = "Poverty Ratio",
                     "RIDAGEYR" = "Age",
                     "LBDHDD"   = "HDL", 
                     "LBXTC"    = "Total Chol.", 
                     "LBDLDL"   = "LDL",
                     "hg" = "Hemoglobin",
                     "tbili" = "Total Bili.",
                     "BMXHT" = "Height",
                     "tprot" = "Total Prot.",
                     "ast" = "AST",
                     "alt" = "ALT",
                     "mcv" = "MCV",
                     "potas" = "Potassium",
                     "pulse" = "Heart Rate",
                     "wbc" = "WBC count",
                     "cal" = "Calcium",
                     "rbc" = "RBC count",
                     "chlor" = "Chloride",
                     "alp" = "Alk. Phos.",
                     "BMXWT" = "Weight",
                     "plt" = "Platelets",
                     "bicarb" = "Bicarbonate"
                     
                    )




sn.shap.nh <-xgb.ggplot.shap.summary(as.matrix(test_model1[,feature_cols.noFPG]), contr.full, model = xgb_model1, top_n = 20) + 
  scale_x_discrete("Feature",labels=renamed_labels) +
  ylab("SHAP value") + labs(color="Feature\nvalue")  + theme.1 +  labs(tag="A") + 
   scale_color_gradient(low="black",high="white",limits=c(-10,10),breaks=c(-10,10),labels=c('Low','High'),na.value = 'black')  + theme(legend.position =c(.75,.35),
                              plot.tag=element_text(size=8),
                              axis.text=element_text(size=8),
```
