```{r Shap Build A1c+}
                              axis.title=element_text(size=8),
                              legend.text = element_text(size=8),
                              legend.title = element_text(size=8),
                              legend.key.size = unit(.05, "in"), 
                              plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5,size=8)) + theme_bw();print(sn.shap.nh)

###############
d.dppos.1 <- xgb.DMatrix(
  data  = as.matrix(df_dppos_aligned[, feature_cols.noFPG]),
  label = df_dppos_aligned$diabetes_flag
)
contr.full.dp.1 <- predict(xgb_model1, d.dppos.1, predcontrib = TRUE)

sn.shap.dp <-xgb.ggplot.shap.summary(as.matrix(df_dppos_aligned[,feature_cols.noFPG]), contr.full.dp.1, model = xgb_model1, top_n = 20) + 
  scale_x_discrete("Feature",labels=renamed_labels) +
  ylab("SHAP value") + labs(color="Feature\nvalue")  + theme.1 +  labs(tag="A") + 
  scale_color_gradient(low="black",high="white",limits=c(-10,10),breaks=c(-10,10),labels=c('Low','High'),na.value = 'black')  + theme(legend.position =c(.75,.35),
                              plot.tag=element_text(size=8),
                              axis.text=element_text(size=8),
                              axis.title=element_text(size=8),
                              legend.text = element_text(size=8),
                              legend.title = element_text(size=8),
                              legend.key.size = unit(.05, "in"), 
                              plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5,size=8))+ theme_bw();print(sn.shap.dp)

#ggsave(sn.shap,filename="~/Dropbox/Narula_Shap.png",width=1.83,height=1.55,units=c("in"))


```



```{r Shap Build A1c/FPG+}

contr.full <- predict(xgb_model2, dtest.2, predcontrib = TRUE)
shap.dat.full<-xgb.plot.shap(as.matrix(test_model1[,feature_cols]), contr.full, model = xgb_model2, top_n = 20, n_col = 5)

theme.1 <- theme(axis.text=element_text(size=8),
            axis.title=element_text(size=12),
            plot.tag.position = c(0.01,0.99),
            plot.tag = element_text(size=20),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5,size=18)) 

feature_cols

sn.shap.nh.2 <-xgb.ggplot.shap.summary(as.matrix(test_model1[,feature_cols]), contr.full, model = xgb_model2, top_n = 20) + 
  scale_x_discrete("Feature",labels=renamed_labels) +
  ylab("SHAP value") + labs(color="Feature\nvalue")  + theme.1 +  labs(tag="A") + 
   scale_color_gradient(low="black",high="white",limits=c(-10,10),breaks=c(-10,10),labels=c('Low','High'),na.value = 'black')  + theme(legend.position =c(.75,.35),
                              plot.tag=element_text(size=8),
                              axis.text=element_text(size=8),
                              axis.title=element_text(size=8),
                              legend.text = element_text(size=8),
                              legend.title = element_text(size=8),
                              legend.key.size = unit(.05, "in"), 
                              plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5,size=8)) + theme_bw();print(sn.shap.nh.2)

###############
d.dppos.2 <- xgb.DMatrix(
  data  = as.matrix(df_dppos_aligned[, feature_cols]),
  label = df_dppos_aligned$diabetes_flag
)
contr.full.dp.2 <- predict(xgb_model2, d.dppos.2, predcontrib = TRUE)

sn.shap.dp.2 <-xgb.ggplot.shap.summary(as.matrix(df_dppos_aligned[,feature_cols]), contr.full.dp.2, model = xgb_model2, top_n = 20) + 
  scale_x_discrete("Feature",labels=renamed_labels) +
  ylab("SHAP value") + labs(color="Feature\nvalue")  + theme.1 +  labs(tag="A") + 
  scale_color_gradient(low="black",high="white",limits=c(-10,10),breaks=c(-10,10),labels=c('Low','High'),na.value = 'black')  + theme(legend.position =c(.75,.35),
                              plot.tag=element_text(size=8),
                              axis.text=element_text(size=8),
                              axis.title=element_text(size=8),
                              legend.text = element_text(size=8),
                              legend.title = element_text(size=8),
                              legend.key.size = unit(.05, "in"), 
                              plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5,size=8))+ theme_bw();print(sn.shap.dp.2)

#ggsave(sn.shap,filename="~/Dropbox/Narula_Shap.png",width=1.83,height=1.55,units=c("in"))


```






```{r}
library(patchwork)
library(scales)
# Define the shared sqrt scale and theme adjustments
# We use trans = "sqrt" to handle the dominant top feature
shared_scales <- list(
  # Use modulus transformation (lambda = 0.5 is square root)
  # This handles negative and positive values symmetrically
  scale_y_continuous("SHAP value (Impact on Prediction)", 
                     trans = modulus_trans(0.5), 
                     breaks = c(-9, -4, -1, 0, 1, 4, 9, 16)), 
  
  scale_color_gradient(low="black", high="grey80", 
                       limits=c(-5,5), breaks=c(-5,5), 
                       labels=c('Low','High'), na.value = 'black'),
  theme_bw(),
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 8)
  )
)

# Apply scales and remove labels from the "Right" panels (B and D)
p_a <- sn.shap.nh   + shared_scales + labs(title = "NHANES A1c+ Validation")
p_b <- sn.shap.dp   + shared_scales + labs(title = "DPPOS A1c+ Validation") + 
       theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank())

p_c <- sn.shap.nh.2 + shared_scales + labs(title = "NHANES A1c/FPG+ Validation")
p_d <- sn.shap.dp.2 + shared_scales + labs(title = "DPPOS A1c/FPG+ Validation") + 
       theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank())



# Combine the four plots
# ( (A | B) / (C | D) ) 
final_shap_grid <- ( (p_a | p_b) / (p_c | p_d) ) + 
  plot_layout(
    guides = "collect", 
    widths = c(1.3, 1) # This forces the internal plotting areas to align
  ) + 
  plot_annotation(
    tag_levels = 'A',
    title = "SHAP Summary of Predictive Features"#,
    #subtitle = "Top Row: A1c+ | Bottom Row: A1c/FPG+"
  ) & 
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12)
  )

# Display
print(final_shap_grid)

ggsave("~/Dropbox/NHANES manuscript/Figure2_SHAP_4Panel.tiff", 
       final_shap_grid, 
       width = 7, height = 9, 
       units = "in", dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")
```





```{r}
theme.1 <- theme(axis.text=element_text(size=8),
            axis.title=element_text(size=12),
            plot.tag.position = c(0.01,0.99),
            plot.tag = element_text(size=20),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5,size=18)) 

office_test <- data.frame(pred_prob2,diabetes_flag = test_model1$diabetes_flag)
plt.calibration.quantile<- office_test %>%  mutate(bin=cut_number(pred_prob2, n = 20)) %>% group_by(bin) %>% reframe(med=median(pred_prob2),freq=sum(diabetes_flag==TRUE)/sum(!is.na(diabetes_flag))) %>% ggplot() + geom_point(aes(x=med,y=freq)) + geom_line(data=data.frame(x=c(0,1),y=c(0,1)),aes(x=x,y=y),linetype="dashed") +  geom_rug(data=office_test,aes(x=pred_prob2,y=as.integer(diabetes_flag)),alpha=0.6) + xlab("Median of Probaility Quantile") + ylab("Frequency of Diabetes Diagnosis") + xlim(0,1) + ylim(0,1) + ggtitle("Frequency of Diagnosis by Quantile for A1c/FPG+ Model") + theme.1; plot(plt.calibration.quantile)

ggsave(file="~/Dropbox/NHANES manuscript/a1cfpg-calibrationplot.tiff",plot=plt.calibration.quantile)
```


```{r}
glm_both
```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Create a dense grid of values for both variables
# We use small steps to make the heatmap look smooth
hba1c_seq <- seq(from = 5.6, to = 6.5, by = 0.1)     # HbA1c range
glucose_seq <- seq(from = 110, to = 126, by = 1)       # Fasting Glucose range

# 2. Generate all possible combinations of these values
heatmap_data <- expand.grid(LBXGH = hba1c_seq, LBXGLU = glucose_seq)

# 3. Predict the probability for every single combination on the grid
# Assuming your model is still named 'my_model'
heatmap_data$predicted_prob <- predict(glm_both, newdata = heatmap_data, type = "response")

# 4. Plot the heatmap
plt.a1cfpg.heat <- ggplot(heatmap_data, aes(x = LBXGH, y = LBXGLU, fill = predicted_prob)) +
  geom_tile() +
  # Using a color gradient where low risk is blue and high risk is red
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#4575b4", mid = "#ffffbf", high = "#d73027", 
    midpoint = 0.5, # Centers the yellow color right at the 50% probability mark
    name = "Probability\n(0 to 1)"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Heatmap of Predicted Diabetes Probability",
    subtitle = "Risk transitions from blue (low) to red (high)",
    x = "Hemoglobin A1c (%)",
    y = "Fasting Plasma Glucose (mg/dL)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  );plot(plt.a1cfpg.heat)

ggsave(plot=plt.a1cfpg.heat,filename = "~/Dropbox/NHANES manuscript/A1cFPG-Heatmap.tiff",width=7,height=7,units=c("in"))
```


```{r}



```



```{r}
beep()
```
